question.english: How do people in your country feel about art?
question.chinese: 你國家的人對藝術有甚麼看法？

source_lines.text: Topic: How do people in your country feel about art?
你國家的人對藝術有甚麼看法？

components.english: In my country, I’d say most people respect art and see it as an important part of culture, even if not everyone is deeply involved in it.
components.chinese: 在我的國家，我會說大多數人都尊重藝術，並且把它視為文化中重要的一部分，即使不是每一個人都會很深入地參與其中。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In my country, I’d say most people respect art and see it as an important
part of culture, even if not everyone is deeply involved in it.
[在我的國家，我會說大多數人都尊重藝術，並且把它視為文化中重要的一部分，即使
不是每一個人都會很深入地參與其中。]

components.english: Art is often seen as something that gives a society character, depth, and emotional colour. It is not just about paintings in galleries; it also includes music, film, design, literature, and traditional crafts. In that sense, art is woven into the fabric of everyday life, even when people do not stop to think about it. Many people may not talk about art every day, but they still appreciate the beauty, identity, and meaning it brings.
components.chinese: 藝術往往被視為一種能賦予社會個性、深度和情感色彩的存在。它不只是美術館裡的畫作，也包括音樂、電影、設計、文學，以及傳統工藝。從這個角度來看，藝術其實早已織進日常生活的紋理之中，即使人們未必時常停下來仔細思考它。很多人未必每天都會談論藝術，但他們仍然欣賞藝術所帶來的美感、身份認同和意義。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Art is often seen as something that gives a society character,
depth, and emotional colour. It is not just about paintings in galleries; it also
includes music, film, design, literature, and traditional crafts. In that sense, art
is woven into the fabric of everyday life, even when people do not stop to think
about it. Many people may not talk about art every day, but they still appreciate
the beauty, identity, and meaning it brings.
[藝術往往被視為一種能賦予社會個性、深度和情感色彩的存在。它不只是美術館裡
的畫作，也包括音樂、電影、設計、文學，以及傳統工藝。從這個角度來看，藝術其實早
已織進日常生活的紋理之中，即使人們未必時常停下來仔細思考它。很多人未必每天
都會談論藝術，但他們仍然欣賞藝術所帶來的美感、身份認同和意義。]

components.english: For example, people may enjoy going to concerts, watching local films, visiting exhibitions, or taking photos of public art without necessarily calling themselves “art lovers”. Even so, those experiences still show that art strikes a chord with them and adds something valuable to daily life.
components.chinese: 例如，人們可能會喜歡去聽演唱會、看本地電影、參觀展覽，或者拍攝公共藝術作品，即使他們未必會把自己稱為「藝術愛好者」。即便如此，這些經驗仍然顯示藝術能夠觸動他們，並且為日常生活增添珍貴的價值。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, people may enjoy going to concerts, watching local
films, visiting exhibitions, or taking photos of public art without necessarily
calling themselves “art lovers”. Even so, those experiences still show that art
strikes a chord with them and adds something valuable to daily life.
[例如，人們可能會喜歡去聽演唱會、看本地電影、參觀展覽，或者拍攝公共藝術作品
，即使他們未必會把自己稱為「藝術愛好者」。即便如此，這些經驗仍然顯示藝術能夠
觸動他們，並且為日常生活增添珍貴的價值。]

components.english: So overall, I would say people in my country generally have a positive attitude towards art, because they see it as a meaningful part of cultural life.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，我會說我國家的人普遍對藝術抱持正面態度，因為他們把藝術視為文化生活中有意義的一部分。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So overall, I would say people in my country generally have a
positive attitude towards art, because they see it as a meaningful part of cultural
life.
[所以，整體來說，我會說我國家的人普遍對藝術抱持正面態度，因為他們把藝術視為
文化生活中有意義的一部分。]

components.english: That said, I also think many people see art as important in principle, but not always as a top priority in practice.
components.chinese: 不過，我也認為很多人雖然在原則上覺得藝術很重要，但在現實中未必會把它放在最優先的位置。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I also think many people see art as important in principle,
but not always as a top priority in practice.
[不過，我也認為很多人雖然在原則上覺得藝術很重要，但在現實中未必會把它放在
最優先的位置。]

components.english: The reason is that modern life is often fast-paced and highly competitive, so practical concerns like work, exams, and financial pressure tend to take centre stage. As a result, art can sometimes take a back seat and be seen as more of a luxury than a necessity. To be honest, some people still think art is not everyone’s cup of tea, especially if they are more focused on careers or academic success. Even so, I do not think that means they look down on it; it is more that life gets in the way.
components.chinese: 原因是，現代生活往往節奏很快，而且競爭激烈，所以像工作、考試，以及經濟壓力這些現實問題通常會佔據最核心的位置。結果就是，藝術有時會被放到次要位置，甚至被看成比較像奢侈品，而不是必需品。老實說，有些人仍然會覺得藝術不是每個人都會特別喜歡的東西，特別是那些更專注於事業或學業成就的人。即便如此，我並不認為這代表他們看不起藝術；更多時候，只是現實生活把注意力拉走了。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: The reason is that modern life is often fast-paced and highly
competitive, so practical concerns like work, exams, and financial pressure tend
to take centre stage. As a result, art can sometimes take a back seat and be seen as
more of a luxury than a necessity. To be honest, some people still think art is not
everyone’s cup of tea, especially if they are more focused on careers or academic
success. Even so, I do not think that means they look down on it; it is more that
life gets in the way.
[原因是，現代生活往往節奏很快，而且競爭激烈，所以像工作、考試，以及經濟壓力
這些現實問題通常會佔據最核心的位置。結果就是，藝術有時會被放到次要位置，甚
至被看成比較像奢侈品，而不是必需品。老實說，有些人仍然會覺得藝術不是每個人
都會特別喜歡的東西，特別是那些更專注於事業或學業成就的人。即便如此，我並不
認為這代表他們看不起藝術；更多時候，只是現實生活把注意力拉走了。]

components.english: For instance, parents may encourage their children to study medicine, law, or business rather than fine arts, simply because those paths seem more secure. However, the same families may still enjoy music, movies, festivals, and beautiful architecture. So there is a slight contradiction there: art is admired, but it does not always come first.
components.chinese: 例如，父母可能會鼓勵孩子讀醫科、法律，或者商科，而不是純藝術，單純因為那些道路看起來更穩妥。不過，同樣的家庭仍然可能很喜歡音樂、電影、節慶，以及美麗的建築。所以，這裡其實存在一點矛盾：人們欣賞藝術，但它未必總是排在最前面。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, parents may encourage their children to study
medicine, law, or business rather than fine arts, simply because those paths seem
more secure. However, the same families may still enjoy music, movies, festivals,
and beautiful architecture. So there is a slight contradiction there: art is
admired, but it does not always come first.
[例如，父母可能會鼓勵孩子讀醫科、法律，或者商科，而不是純藝術，單純因為那些
道路看起來更穩妥。不過，同樣的家庭仍然可能很喜歡音樂、電影、節慶，以及美麗的
建築。所以，這裡其實存在一點矛盾：人們欣賞藝術，但它未必總是排在最前面。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people in my country value art, but their relationship with it is a mixed one: they admire it, enjoy it, and benefit from it, even though practical pressures sometimes stop it from being front and centre.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說我國家的人是重視藝術的，只不過他們和藝術之間的關係有點複雜：他們欣賞藝術、享受藝術，也從中受益，只是現實壓力有時令藝術無法成為生活中最核心的部分。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people in my country value art, but their
relationship with it is a mixed one: they admire it, enjoy it, and benefit from it,
even though practical pressures sometimes stop it from being front and centre.
[總結來說，我會說我國家的人是重視藝術的，只不過他們和藝術之間的關係有點複
雜：他們欣賞藝術、享受藝術，也從中受益，只是現實壓力有時令藝術無法成為生活中
最核心的部分。]

question.english: Do people in your country prefer music over art?
question.chinese: 你國家的人是否比起藝術更喜歡音樂？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do people in your country prefer music over art?
你國家的人是否比起藝術更喜歡音樂？

components.english: If we are using “art” to mean things like painting, sculpture, or gallery-based visual art, I would say that many people in my country probably do prefer music.
components.chinese: 如果這裡的「藝術」是指繪畫、雕塑，或者美術館裡的視覺藝術，那我會說，在我的國家，很多人可能的確比較喜歡音樂。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: If we are using “art” to mean things like painting, sculpture, or
gallery-based visual art, I would say that many people in my country probably
do prefer music.
[如果這裡的「藝術」是指繪畫、雕塑，或者美術館裡的視覺藝術，那我會說，在我的國
家，很多人可能的確比較喜歡音樂。]

components.english: The main reason is that music is far more woven into everyday life. People can listen to it while commuting, exercising, studying, or relaxing, so it is always within arm’s reach. Visual art, by contrast, often requires people to slow down, pay closer attention, and sometimes make a special effort to see it. In other words, music slips naturally into daily routines, whereas visual art is more something people have to seek out.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，音樂和日常生活的連結明顯更緊密。人們在通勤、做運動、溫習，或者放鬆時都可以聽音樂，所以它幾乎隨手可得。相比之下，視覺藝術通常需要人們放慢腳步、更專注地欣賞，有時甚至要特地花心思去接觸。換句話說，音樂很自然地流進了日常生活，而視覺藝術則更像是人們需要主動去接近的東西。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that music is far more woven into everyday
life. People can listen to it while commuting, exercising, studying, or relaxing, so
it is always within arm’s reach. Visual art, by contrast, often requires people to
slow down, pay closer attention, and sometimes make a special effort to see it. In
other words, music slips naturally into daily routines, whereas visual art is more
something people have to seek out.
[主要原因是，音樂和日常生活的連結明顯更緊密。人們在通勤、做運動、溫習，或者
放鬆時都可以聽音樂，所以它幾乎隨手可得。相比之下，視覺藝術通常需要人們放慢
腳步、更專注地欣賞，有時甚至要特地花心思去接觸。換句話說，音樂很自然地流進了
日常生活，而視覺藝術則更像是人們需要主動去接近的東西。]

components.english: For example, most people may listen to songs every single day on their phones, but they do not necessarily visit exhibitions or art museums very often. That does not mean they dislike art; it simply means music is easier to consume and easier to connect with on the spur of the moment.
components.chinese: 例如，大多數人可能每天都會用手機聽歌，但未必會經常去看展覽或參觀美術館。這並不代表他們不喜歡藝術；只是音樂更容易接觸，也更容易在一時一刻之間產生共鳴。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, most people may listen to songs every single day on
their phones, but they do not necessarily visit exhibitions or art museums very
often. That does not mean they dislike art; it simply means music is easier to
consume and easier to connect with on the spur of the moment.
[例如，大多數人可能每天都會用手機聽歌，但未必會經常去看展覽或參觀美術館。這
並不代表他們不喜歡藝術；只是音樂更容易接觸，也更容易在一時一刻之間產生共

components.english: So, in practical terms, I would say music tends to be more popular because it is more accessible, more immediate, and more deeply embedded in daily life.
components.chinese: 所以，從現實層面來看，我會說音樂通常更受歡迎，因為它更容易接觸、更直接，也更深地融入了日常生活。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in practical terms, I would say music tends to be more
popular because it is more accessible, more immediate, and more deeply
embedded in daily life.
[所以，從現實層面來看，我會說音樂通常更受歡迎，因為它更容易接觸、更直接，也
更深地融入了日常生活。]

components.english: That said, I do not think this means people value music more deeply than art in every sense, because visual art still plays an important cultural and emotional role.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為這就代表人們在所有層面上都比起藝術更重視音樂，因為視覺藝術仍然扮演著重要的文化和情感角色。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think this means people value music more deeply
than art in every sense, because visual art still plays an important cultural and
emotional role.
[不過，我並不認為這就代表人們在所有層面上都比起藝術更重視音樂，因為視覺藝
術仍然扮演著重要的文化和情感角色。]

components.english: Art may not be as omnipresent as music, but it still leaves a strong mark on society through design, architecture, film, public spaces, and cultural heritage. In a way, music may be the soundtrack of everyday life, while visual art is part of the landscape people live in. So the difference is not always about preference alone; sometimes it is about how people experience each form.
components.chinese: 藝術或許不像音樂那樣無處不在，但它仍然透過設計、建築、電影、公共空間，以及文化遺產，在社會上留下深刻印記。某程度上，音樂像是日常生活的配樂，而視覺藝術則是人們所處環境的一部分。所以，兩者的差別未必只是單純的喜好問題；有時更在於人們是如何體驗每一種形式。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Art may not be as omnipresent as music, but it still leaves a
strong mark on society through design, architecture, film, public spaces, and
cultural heritage. In a way, music may be the soundtrack of everyday life, while
visual art is part of the landscape people live in. So the difference is not always
about preference alone; sometimes it is about how people experience each form.
[藝術或許不像音樂那樣無處不在，但它仍然透過設計、建築、電影、公共空間，以及
文化遺產，在社會上留下深刻印記。某程度上，音樂像是日常生活的配樂，而視覺藝術
則是人們所處環境的一部分。所以，兩者的差別未必只是單純的喜好問題；有時更在
於人們是如何體驗每一種形式。]

components.english: For instance, people may not talk about paintings every day, but they still admire beautiful buildings, enjoy well-designed spaces, and take pride in traditional artistic culture. So even if music wins people’s ears more easily, art still speaks to them in quieter but lasting ways.
components.chinese: 例如，人們未必每天都會談論畫作，但他們仍然會欣賞美麗的建築、享受設計出色的空間，並且為傳統藝術文化感到自豪。所以，即使音樂更容易先抓住人們的耳朵，藝術仍然會以較安靜卻長久的方式打動他們。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, people may not talk about paintings every day, but
they still admire beautiful buildings, enjoy well-designed spaces, and take pride
in traditional artistic culture. So even if music wins people’s ears more easily, art
still speaks to them in quieter but lasting ways.
[例如，人們未必每天都會談論畫作，但他們仍然會欣賞美麗的建築、享受設計出色的
空間，並且為傳統藝術文化感到自豪。所以，即使音樂更容易先抓住人們的耳朵，藝術
仍然會以較安靜卻長久的方式打動他們。]

components.english: Overall, I would say many people in my country probably prefer music in everyday life, but that does not mean art is pushed to the margins. It is more a case of music being easier to enjoy instantly, whereas art often works its magic more slowly.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，在我的國家，很多人在日常生活中大概會比較偏向音樂，但這並不代表藝術被擠到邊緣。更準確地說，只是音樂更容易立刻被享受，而藝術往往是慢慢發揮它的魅力。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say many people in my country probably
prefer music in everyday life, but that does not mean art is pushed to the
margins. It is more a case of music being easier to enjoy instantly, whereas art
often works its magic more slowly.
[總結來說，我會說，在我的國家，很多人在日常生活中大概會比較偏向音樂，但這並
不代表藝術被擠到邊緣。更準確地說，只是音樂更容易立刻被享受，而藝術往往是慢
慢發揮它的魅力。]

question.english: What are some traditional art forms in your country?
question.chinese: 傳統藝術形式

source_lines.text: Topic: What are some traditional art forms in your country?
傳統藝術形式

components.english: In Hong Kong, some of the most traditional art forms are Cantonese opera and lion dance, especially in festivals and community celebrations.
components.chinese: 在香港，其中一些最具代表性的傳統藝術形式是粵劇和舞獅，尤其常見於節慶和社區慶祝活動中。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In Hong Kong, some of the most traditional art forms are Cantonese
opera and lion dance, especially in festivals and community celebrations.
[在香港，其中一些最具代表性的傳統藝術形式是粵劇和舞獅，尤其常見於節慶和社
區慶祝活動中。]

components.english: Cantonese opera is probably the crown jewel of traditional performance art in Hong Kong, because it brings together singing, acting, stylised movement, martial elements, and elaborate costumes. Lion dance is different, but just as iconic. It combines rhythm, energy, symbolism, and performance in a way that can bring a whole street to life. In a sense, these art forms are like living museums: they do not simply preserve tradition; they perform it in front of people’s eyes.
components.chinese: 粵劇大概可以說是香港傳統表演藝術中的明珠，因為它結合了歌唱、表演、程式化動作、武打元素，以及華麗的戲服。舞獅則屬於另一種形式，但同樣極具代表性。它把節奏、活力、象徵意義和表演性融合在一起，往往能令整條街都熱鬧起來。某程度上，這些藝術形式就像活的博物館：它們不只是保存傳統，而是把傳統直接演在人的眼前。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Cantonese opera is probably the crown jewel of traditional
performance art in Hong Kong, because it brings together singing, acting,
stylised movement, martial elements, and elaborate costumes. Lion dance is
different, but just as iconic. It combines rhythm, energy, symbolism, and
performance in a way that can bring a whole street to life. In a sense, these art
forms are like living museums: they do not simply preserve tradition; they
perform it in front of people’s eyes.
[粵劇大概可以說是香港傳統表演藝術中的明珠，因為它結合了歌唱、表演、程式化動
作、武打元素，以及華麗的戲服。舞獅則屬於另一種形式，但同樣極具代表性。它把節
奏、活力、象徵意義和表演性融合在一起，往往能令整條街都熱鬧起來。某程度上，這
些藝術形式就像活的博物館：它們不只是保存傳統，而是把傳統直接演在人的眼前。]

components.english: For example, during Lunar New Year or temple festivals, people often see lion dances outside shops, restaurants, or community venues. At the same time, Cantonese opera can still be performed in theatres or during festive events, and even people who are not regular fans usually recognise it straight away.
components.chinese: 例如，在農曆新年或廟會期間，人們經常會在店舖、酒樓或社區場所外面看到舞獅表演。與此同時，粵劇至今仍會在劇院或節慶活動中上演，而即使不是經常欣賞的人，通常也能一眼認出它。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, during Lunar New Year or temple festivals, people
often see lion dances outside shops, restaurants, or community venues. At the
same time, Cantonese opera can still be performed in theatres or during festive
events, and even people who are not regular fans usually recognise it straight
away.
[例如，在農曆新年或廟會期間，人們經常會在店舖、酒樓或社區場所外面看到舞獅表
演。與此同時，粵劇至今仍會在劇院或節慶活動中上演，而即使不是經常欣賞的人，通
常也能一眼認出它。]

components.english: So, when people talk about traditional art in Hong Kong, performance arts like Cantonese opera and lion dance are usually front and centre.
components.chinese: 所以，當人們談到香港的傳統藝術時，像粵劇和舞獅這類表演藝術通常都會最先被提起。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, when people talk about traditional art in Hong Kong,
performance arts like Cantonese opera and lion dance are usually front and
centre.
[所以，當人們談到香港的傳統藝術時，像粵劇和舞獅這類表演藝術通常都會最先被
提起。]

components.english: On top of that, Hong Kong also values traditional visual arts and crafts, such as Chinese calligraphy, ink painting, paper cutting, and lantern-making.
components.chinese: 除此之外，香港也很重視傳統視覺藝術和工藝，例如中國書法、水墨畫、剪紙，以及燈籠製作。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, Hong Kong also values traditional visual arts and
crafts, such as Chinese calligraphy, ink painting, paper cutting, and
lantern-making.
[除此之外，香港也很重視傳統視覺藝術和工藝，例如中國書法、水墨畫、剪紙，以及
燈籠製作。]

components.english: These forms may be quieter than stage performances, but they carry culture in a more delicate and lasting way. Calligraphy is not just about writing neatly; it is often seen as an art of discipline, balance, and inner expression. Ink painting works in a similar way, because a few brushstrokes can sometimes say more than a page of explanation. Traditional crafts, meanwhile, keep culture close to everyday life, especially during seasonal festivals.
components.chinese: 這些形式也許不像舞台表演那樣熱鬧，但它們以更細膩、更持久的方式承載文化。書法並不只是把字寫得工整；它往往也被視為一種講求修養、平衡和內在表達的藝術。水墨畫也有類似的特質，因為有時只是寥寥幾筆，已經勝過長篇解說。至於傳統工藝，則特別能把文化帶回日常生活之中，尤其是在節慶時節。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: These forms may be quieter than stage performances, but they
carry culture in a more delicate and lasting way. Calligraphy is not just about
writing neatly; it is often seen as an art of discipline, balance, and inner
expression. Ink painting works in a similar way, because a few brushstrokes can
sometimes say more than a page of explanation. Traditional crafts, meanwhile,
keep culture close to everyday life, especially during seasonal festivals.
[這些形式也許不像舞台表演那樣熱鬧，但它們以更細膩、更持久的方式承載文化。書
法並不只是把字寫得工整；它往往也被視為一種講求修養、平衡和內在表達的藝術。
水墨畫也有類似的特質，因為有時只是寥寥幾筆，已經勝過長篇解說。至於傳統工藝
，則特別能把文化帶回日常生活之中，尤其是在節慶時節。]

components.english: For instance, during Mid-Autumn Festival or Lunar New Year, traditional decorations and handmade crafts often appear in schools, markets, temples, and public displays. In the same way, calligraphy and ink painting are still taught in some classes and practised by people who want to keep that heritage alive.
components.chinese: 例如，在中秋節或農曆新年期間，傳統裝飾和手工藝常常會出現在學校、市集、廟宇，以及各種公共展示之中。同樣地，書法和水墨畫至今仍會在一些課堂中教授，也有人會持續練習，希望把這份文化傳承下去。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, during Mid-Autumn Festival or Lunar New Year,
traditional decorations and handmade crafts often appear in schools, markets,
temples, and public displays. In the same way, calligraphy and ink painting are
still taught in some classes and practised by people who want to keep that
heritage alive.
[例如，在中秋節或農曆新年期間，傳統裝飾和手工藝常常會出現在學校、市集、廟宇
，以及各種公共展示之中。同樣地，書法和水墨畫至今仍會在一些課堂中教授，也有人
會持續練習，希望把這份文化傳承下去。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say Hong Kong’s traditional art forms range from dramatic performance arts to refined visual crafts. What makes them special is that they are not just old traditions on paper; they are still part of the city’s cultural heartbeat.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說香港的傳統藝術形式涵蓋了充滿戲劇感的表演藝術，也包括細緻典雅的視覺工藝。它們之所以特別，在於它們並不只是紙上的古老傳統，而仍然是這座城市文化脈搏的一部分。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say Hong Kong’s traditional art forms range from
dramatic performance arts to refined visual crafts. What makes them special is
that they are not just old traditions on paper; they are still part of the city’s
cultural heartbeat.
[總結來說，我會說香港的傳統藝術形式涵蓋了充滿戲劇感的表演藝術，也包括細緻
典雅的視覺工藝。它們之所以特別，在於它們並不只是紙上的古老傳統，而仍然是這
座城市文化脈搏的一部分。]

question.english: How has art changed in the past few decades in your country?
question.chinese: 在過去幾十年裡，你國家的藝術有甚麼變化？

source_lines.text: Topic: How has art changed in the past few decades in your country?
在過去幾十年裡，你國家的藝術有甚麼變化？

components.english: In Hong Kong, I’d say art has become far more diverse and contemporary than it used to be.
components.chinese: 在香港，我會說藝術比起從前已經變得更多元，也更當代化。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In Hong Kong, I’d say art has become far more diverse and
contemporary than it used to be.
[在香港，我會說藝術比起從前已經變得更多元，也更當代化。]

components.english: A few decades ago, people were more likely to associate art with traditional forms such as ink painting, calligraphy, craftwork, or opera. Those still matter, of course, but the picture is much broader now. These days, Hong Kong’s art scene also includes photography, moving image, installation, design, architecture, and digital work. In a way, art has moved from the frame to the screen, and sometimes even into the street.
components.chinese: 幾十年前，人們較容易把藝術和水墨畫、書法、工藝，或者戲曲這類較傳統的形式連在一起。當然，這些形式今天仍然重要，但整體畫面已經寬廣得多了。如今，香港的藝術圈同時也包括攝影、影像藝術、裝置藝術、設計、建築，以及數碼作品。某程度上，藝術已經由畫框裡走到螢幕上，甚至走進了街頭。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: A few decades ago, people were more likely to associate art with
traditional forms such as ink painting, calligraphy, craftwork, or opera. Those
still matter, of course, but the picture is much broader now. These days, Hong
Kong’s art scene also includes photography, moving image, installation, design,
architecture, and digital work. In a way, art has moved from the frame to the
screen, and sometimes even into the street.
[幾十年前，人們較容易把藝術和水墨畫、書法、工藝，或者戲曲這類較傳統的形式連
在一起。當然，這些形式今天仍然重要，但整體畫面已經寬廣得多了。如今，香港的藝
術圈同時也包括攝影、影像藝術、裝置藝術、設計、建築，以及數碼作品。某程度上，藝
術已經由畫框裡走到螢幕上，甚至走進了街頭。]

components.english: For example, if you compare older exhibition culture with places like M+ or contemporary programmes at Tai Kwun, you can clearly see that art in Hong Kong is now much more experimental and cross-disciplinary. People are no longer just looking at paintings on a wall; they may also walk into immersive installations or engage with video-based work.
components.chinese: 例如，如果你把以往較傳統的展覽文化，和像 M+ 或大館的當代藝術項目相比，就會很清楚看到，香港的藝術現在已經變得更具實驗性，也更跨界。人們不再只是站在牆前看畫；他們也可能走進沉浸式裝置，或者接觸以影像為主的作品。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if you compare older exhibition culture with places
like M+ or contemporary programmes at Tai Kwun, you can clearly see that art
in Hong Kong is now much more experimental and cross-disciplinary. People
are no longer just looking at paintings on a wall; they may also walk into
immersive installations or engage with video-based work.
[例如，如果你把以往較傳統的展覽文化，和像 M+ 或大館的當代藝術項目相比，就會
很清楚看到，香港的藝術現在已經變得更具實驗性，也更跨界。人們不再只是站在牆
前看畫；他們也可能走進沉浸式裝置，或者接觸以影像為主的作品。]

components.english: So, the first big change is that art in Hong Kong has expanded enormously in terms of medium, style, and the way people experience it.
components.chinese: 所以，第一個很明顯的變化，就是香港的藝術在媒介、風格，以及人們體驗它的方式上，都大大擴展了。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, the first big change is that art in Hong Kong has expanded
enormously in terms of medium, style, and the way people experience it.
[所以，第一個很明顯的變化，就是香港的藝術在媒介、風格，以及人們體驗它的方式
上，都大大擴展了。]

components.english: Another major change is that art in Hong Kong has become more international, more public-facing, and more woven into everyday city life.
components.chinese: 另一個主要變化是，香港的藝術已經變得更國際化、更面向公眾，也更融入城市的日常生活。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another major change is that art in Hong Kong has become more
international, more public-facing, and more woven into everyday city life.
[另一個主要變化是，香港的藝術已經變得更國際化、更面向公眾，也更融入城市的日
常生活。]

components.english: In the past, art could feel a little niche or tucked away, but now it is much more visible. Hong Kong has developed major cultural hubs and international platforms, including West Kowloon, M+, Tai Kwun, and Art Basel Hong Kong. As a result, art no longer stays in a small circle; it has stepped out of the shadows and into the mainstream.
components.chinese: 過去，藝術有時會給人一種比較小眾、比較被收起來的感覺，但現在它明顯更容易被看見了。香港已經發展出一些重要的文化樞紐和國際平台，包括西九文化區、M+、大館，以及香港巴塞爾藝術展。結果就是，藝術不再只是停留在一個小圈子裡，而是逐漸走出陰影，進入主流視野。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: In the past, art could feel a little niche or tucked away, but
now it is much more visible. Hong Kong has developed major cultural hubs and
international platforms, including West Kowloon, M+, Tai Kwun, and Art Basel
Hong Kong. As a result, art no longer stays in a small circle; it has stepped out
of the shadows and into the mainstream.
[過去，藝術有時會給人一種比較小眾、比較被收起來的感覺，但現在它明顯更容易被
看見了。香港已經發展出一些重要的文化樞紐和國際平台，包括西九文化區、M+、大
館，以及香港巴塞爾藝術展。結果就是，藝術不再只是停留在一個小圈子裡，而是逐漸
走出陰影，進入主流視野。]

components.english: For instance, nowadays people in Hong Kong are more likely to come across art not only in museums, but also in heritage venues, festivals, public programmes, and even on neighbourhood walls through street art. That shows art is no longer something people have to chase after; more often than not, it meets them halfway.
components.chinese: 例如，現在的香港人更有可能不只是在博物館裡接觸藝術，也會在歷史建築活化場地、藝術節、公共活動，甚至社區牆上的街頭藝術中遇見它。這正好說明，藝術已經不再是人們必須特地去追尋的東西；很多時候，它反而會主動走近人們。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, nowadays people in Hong Kong are more likely to
come across art not only in museums, but also in heritage venues, festivals,
public programmes, and even on neighbourhood walls through street art. That
shows art is no longer something people have to chase after; more often than
not, it meets them halfway.
[例如，現在的香港人更有可能不只是在博物館裡接觸藝術，也會在歷史建築活化場
地、藝術節、公共活動，甚至社區牆上的街頭藝術中遇見它。這正好說明，藝術已經不
再是人們必須特地去追尋的東西；很多時候，它反而會主動走近人們。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say art in Hong Kong has changed from being relatively traditional and more niche to being more diverse, contemporary, international, and accessible. That said, what makes Hong Kong interesting is that the old and the new still go hand in hand.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說香港的藝術已經由一種相對較傳統、較小眾的狀態，發展成更更多元、更當代、更國際化，也更容易接觸的樣貌。不過，香港最有趣的地方就在於，傳統和創新至今仍然並肩而行。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say art in Hong Kong has changed from being
relatively traditional and more niche to being more diverse, contemporary,
international, and accessible. That said, what makes Hong Kong interesting is
that the old and the new still go hand in hand.
[總結來說，我會說香港的藝術已經由一種相對較傳統、較小眾的狀態，發展成更更多
元、更當代、更國際化，也更容易接觸的樣貌。不過，香港最有趣的地方就在於，傳統
和創新至今仍然並肩而行。]

question.english: Do you think children should study art in school?
question.chinese: 你認為孩子應該在學校學習藝術嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think children should study art in school?
你認為孩子應該在學校學習藝術嗎？

components.english: Yes, I definitely think children should study art in school, because art helps them develop creativity, self-expression, and a more balanced way of thinking.
components.chinese: 是的，我的確認為孩子應該在學校學習藝術，因為藝術能幫助他們培養創造力、自我表達能力，以及一種更全面的思考方式。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I definitely think children should study art in school, because art
helps them develop creativity, self-expression, and a more balanced way of
thinking.
[是的，我的確認為孩子應該在學校學習藝術，因為藝術能幫助他們培養創造力、自我
表達能力，以及一種更全面的思考方式。]

components.english: School should not just be about memorising facts or chasing marks. Children also need space to imagine, explore, and express what is going on in their minds. Art gives them that space. In a way, it opens a window into how they see the world. Some feelings are hard to put into words, especially for younger children, so drawing, painting, or making things with their hands can help them communicate in a more natural way. On top of that, art gives children a little room to breathe in an education system that can sometimes feel too exam-driven.
components.chinese: 學校不應該只圍繞背誦知識或追逐分數。孩子同時也需要空間去想像、探索，以及表達自己內心的想法，而藝術正正能提供這樣的空間。某程度上，它像是打開了一扇窗，讓孩子把自己眼中的世界呈現出來。有些感受很難用言語說清楚，尤其對年紀較小的孩子來說更是如此，所以透過畫畫、上色，或者親手做作品，他們往往能以更自然的方式表達自己。除此之外，在一個有時過於以考試為中心的教育制度裡，藝術也能讓孩子稍微喘一口氣。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: School should not just be about memorising facts or chasing
marks. Children also need space to imagine, explore, and express what is going
on in their minds. Art gives them that space. In a way, it opens a window into
how they see the world. Some feelings are hard to put into words, especially for
younger children, so drawing, painting, or making things with their hands can
help them communicate in a more natural way. On top of that, art gives
children a little room to breathe in an education system that can sometimes feel
too exam-driven.
[學校不應該只圍繞背誦知識或追逐分數。孩子同時也需要空間去想像、探索，以及表
達自己內心的想法，而藝術正正能提供這樣的空間。某程度上，它像是打開了一扇窗，
讓孩子把自己眼中的世界呈現出來。有些感受很難用言語說清楚，尤其對年紀較小的
孩子來說更是如此，所以透過畫畫、上色，或者親手做作品，他們往往能以更自然的方
式表達自己。除此之外，在一個有時過於以考試為中心的教育制度裡，藝術也能讓孩
子稍微喘一口氣。]

components.english: For example, a child who is quiet in class may not speak much during lessons, but they may produce a drawing that says a great deal about their imagination or emotions. In that kind of case, art becomes much more than a school subject; it becomes a voice.
components.chinese: 例如，一個在課堂上比較安靜的孩子，平日未必會說很多話，但他畫出來的一幅畫，卻可能充分展現他的想像力或情感。在這種情況下，藝術就不再只是一門學科，而像是一把屬於他的聲音。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a child who is quiet in class may not speak much
during lessons, but they may produce a drawing that says a great deal about
their imagination or emotions. In that kind of case, art becomes much more
than a school subject; it becomes a voice.
[例如，一個在課堂上比較安靜的孩子，平日未必會說很多話，但他畫出來的一幅畫，
卻可能充分展現他的想像力或情感。在這種情況下，藝術就不再只是一門學科，而像
是一把屬於他的聲音。]

components.english: So yes, I would say art deserves a place in school, because it helps children grow not only academically, but also emotionally and creatively.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說藝術值得在學校裡佔有一席之地，因為它幫助孩子成長的，不只是學業能力，還有情感和創造力。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say art deserves a place in school, because it helps
children grow not only academically, but also emotionally and creatively.
[所以，是的，我會說藝術值得在學校裡佔有一席之地，因為它幫助孩子成長的，不只
是學業能力，還有情感和創造力。]

components.english: Another reason is that studying art can teach children valuable life skills, even if they never become artists in the future.
components.chinese: 另一個原因是，即使孩子將來未必會成為藝術家，學習藝術仍然能教會他們一些非常有價值的人生能力。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another reason is that studying art can teach children valuable life
skills, even if they never become artists in the future.
[另一個原因是，即使孩子將來未必會成為藝術家，學習藝術仍然能教會他們一些非
常有價值的人生能力。]

components.english: Art teaches patience, attention to detail, and the confidence to experiment. It also shows children that not everything has one fixed answer. In many academic subjects, there is a right or wrong response, whereas art allows more freedom and interpretation. That can be incredibly healthy, because real life is rarely black and white. Art also teaches children how to cope when things do not go to plan. Sometimes a mistake in an artwork turns into part of the final piece, which is a lovely reminder that setbacks do not always mean failure.
components.chinese: 藝術能教會孩子耐性、對細節的觀察力，以及勇於嘗試的自信。它同時也讓孩子明白，並不是所有事情都只有一個固定答案。在很多學術科目中，答案往往分對或錯；但藝術容許更多自由和詮釋空間。這其實非常健康，因為現實生活很少是非黑即白的。藝術也能教孩子如何面對事情不如預期時的情況。有時候，一個作品裡的「失誤」最後反而會成為作品的一部分，這其實很美，也提醒人們挫折未必就等於失敗。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Art teaches patience, attention to detail, and the confidence to
experiment. It also shows children that not everything has one fixed answer. In
many academic subjects, there is a right or wrong response, whereas art allows
more freedom and interpretation. That can be incredibly healthy, because real
life is rarely black and white. Art also teaches children how to cope when things
do not go to plan. Sometimes a mistake in an artwork turns into part of the
final piece, which is a lovely reminder that setbacks do not always mean failure.
[藝術能教會孩子耐性、對細節的觀察力，以及勇於嘗試的自信。它同時也讓孩子明白
，並不是所有事情都只有一個固定答案。在很多學術科目中，答案往往分對或錯；但藝
術容許更多自由和詮釋空間。這其實非常健康，因為現實生活很少是非黑即白的。藝
術也能教孩子如何面對事情不如預期時的情況。有時候，一個作品裡的「失誤」最後反
而會成為作品的一部分，這其實很美，也提醒人們挫折未必就等於失敗。]

components.english: For instance, when children work on a painting or a design project, they often have to make choices, solve little problems, and adapt as they go along. Bit by bit, they learn to trust their own judgement instead of being afraid of getting everything wrong.
components.chinese: 例如，當孩子在完成一幅畫或一個設計作品時，他們往往需要作出選擇、處理一些小問題，並且在過程中不斷調整。久而久之，他們就會慢慢學會相信自己的判斷，而不是凡事都害怕做錯。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, when children work on a painting or a design project,
they often have to make choices, solve little problems, and adapt as they go
along. Bit by bit, they learn to trust their own judgement instead of being
afraid of getting everything wrong.
[例如，當孩子在完成一幅畫或一個設計作品時，他們往往需要作出選擇、處理一些小
問題，並且在過程中不斷調整。久而久之，他們就會慢慢學會相信自己的判斷，而不是
凡事都害怕做錯。]

components.english: Overall, I believe children should study art in school, not because every child needs to be an artist, but because art helps them become more expressive, more thoughtful, and more well-rounded as people.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我相信孩子應該在學校學習藝術，不是因為每個孩子都要成為藝術家，而是因為藝術能幫助他們成為更懂表達、更有思考能力，也更全面發展的人。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I believe children should study art in school, not
because every child needs to be an artist, but because art helps them become
more expressive, more thoughtful, and more well-rounded as people.
[總結來說，我相信孩子應該在學校學習藝術，不是因為每個孩子都要成為藝術家，而
是因為藝術能幫助他們成為更懂表達、更有思考能力，也更全面發展的人。]

question.english: How can children benefit from learning about art?
question.chinese: 孩子學習藝術可以得到甚麼好處？

source_lines.text: Topic: How can children benefit from learning about art?
孩子學習藝術可以得到甚麼好處？

components.english: I think children can benefit from learning about art in many ways, but one of the biggest advantages is that it helps them develop creativity and self-expression.
components.chinese: 我認為，孩子學習藝術可以在很多方面受益，而其中一個最大的好處，就是它能幫助他們培養創造力和自我表達能力。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think children can benefit from learning about art in many ways, but
one of the biggest advantages is that it helps them develop creativity and
self-expression.
[我認為，孩子學習藝術可以在很多方面受益，而其中一個最大的好處，就是它能幫助
他們培養創造力和自我表達能力。]

components.english: Art gives children a space to imagine, explore, and express what is on their minds. Not every child finds it easy to explain their feelings in words, so art can become another language for them. Through drawing, painting, music, drama, or design, they learn to think outside the box and show their ideas in a more personal way. In a sense, art opens a window into their inner world.
components.chinese: 藝術讓孩子擁有一個可以想像、探索，以及表達內心想法的空間。並不是每一個孩子都容易用言語說清楚自己的感受，所以藝術可以成為他們的另一種語言。透過繪畫、上色、音樂、戲劇，或者設計，他們會學懂跳出框框思考，並用更具個人特色的方式呈現自己的想法。某程度上，藝術就像打開一扇窗，讓人看見他們的內心世界。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Art gives children a space to imagine, explore, and express what
is on their minds. Not every child finds it easy to explain their feelings in words,
so art can become another language for them. Through drawing, painting,
music, drama, or design, they learn to think outside the box and show their
ideas in a more personal way. In a sense, art opens a window into their inner
world.
[藝術讓孩子擁有一個可以想像、探索，以及表達內心想法的空間。並不是每一個孩子
都容易用言語說清楚自己的感受，所以藝術可以成為他們的另一種語言。透過繪畫、
上色、音樂、戲劇，或者設計，他們會學懂跳出框框思考，並用更具個人特色的方式呈
現自己的想法。某程度上，藝術就像打開一扇窗，讓人看見他們的內心世界。]

components.english: For example, a child who is rather quiet in class may not speak very much, but they may create a picture or a piece of craftwork that says a great deal about how they think and feel. In that case, art is not just a subject; it becomes a voice.
components.chinese: 例如，一個在課堂上比較安靜的孩子，平日未必會說很多話，但他可能透過一幅畫或一件手工藝作品，表達出很多自己的想法和感受。在這種情況下，藝術就不再只是一門學科，而會變成一把屬於他的聲音。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a child who is rather quiet in class may not speak very
much, but they may create a picture or a piece of craftwork that says a great deal
about how they think and feel. In that case, art is not just a subject; it becomes a
voice.
[例如，一個在課堂上比較安靜的孩子，平日未必會說很多話，但他可能透過一幅畫或
一件手工藝作品，表達出很多自己的想法和感受。在這種情況下，藝術就不再只是一
門學科，而會變成一把屬於他的聲音。]

components.english: So, one major benefit of learning about art is that it helps children become more imaginative and more confident in expressing themselves.
components.chinese: 所以，學習藝術其中一個主要好處，就是它能幫助孩子變得更有想像力，也更有自信去表達自己。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major benefit of learning about art is that it helps
children become more imaginative and more confident in expressing themselves.
[所以，學習藝術其中一個主要好處，就是它能幫助孩子變得更有想像力，也更有自信
去表達自己。]

components.english: Another important benefit is that art can teach children patience, observation, and a more open-minded way of seeing the world.
components.chinese: 另一個重要的好處是，藝術能教會孩子耐性、觀察力，以及一種更開放的看世界方式。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another important benefit is that art can teach children patience,
observation, and a more open-minded way of seeing the world.
[另一個重要的好處是，藝術能教會孩子耐性、觀察力，以及一種更開放的看世界方

components.english: When children learn about art, they have to notice details, make choices, and sometimes start again when things do not go to plan. That process teaches resilience and problem-solving. At the same time, art also brings them into contact with different cultures, styles, and perspectives, so it helps them see the world through a different lens. Real life is rarely black and white, and art reminds children of that in a very natural way.
components.chinese: 當孩子學習藝術時，他們需要留意細節、作出選擇，而且有時事情不如預期時，還要重新開始。這個過程能教會他們韌性和解難能力。與此同時，藝術也會讓他們接觸到不同的文化、風格，以及觀點，因此能幫助他們用另一個角度看世界。現實生活很少是非黑即白的，而藝術正正以一種很自然的方式提醒孩子這一點。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: When children learn about art, they have to notice details,
make choices, and sometimes start again when things do not go to plan. That
process teaches resilience and problem-solving. At the same time, art also brings
them into contact with different cultures, styles, and perspectives, so it helps
them see the world through a different lens. Real life is rarely black and white,
and art reminds children of that in a very natural way.
[當孩子學習藝術時，他們需要留意細節、作出選擇，而且有時事情不如預期時，還要
重新開始。這個過程能教會他們韌性和解難能力。與此同時，藝術也會讓他們接觸到
不同的文化、風格，以及觀點，因此能幫助他們用另一個角度看世界。現實生活很少是
非黑即白的，而藝術正正以一種很自然的方式提醒孩子這一點。]

components.english: For instance, if children discuss a painting from another culture or work on a design project together, they learn not only to appreciate beauty, but also to respect different interpretations. Little by little, they realise that there is more than one way to think, and that can stand them in good stead later in life.
components.chinese: 例如，如果孩子一起討論一幅來自另一種文化的畫作，或者合作完成一個設計項目，他們學到的就不只是欣賞美感，還包括尊重不同的理解方式。久而久之，他們會明白，很多事情其實不只得一種想法，而這種能力在日後的人生中會很有幫助。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if children discuss a painting from another culture or
work on a design project together, they learn not only to appreciate beauty, but
also to respect different interpretations. Little by little, they realise that there is
more than one way to think, and that can stand them in good stead later in life.
[例如，如果孩子一起討論一幅來自另一種文化的畫作，或者合作完成一個設計項目，
他們學到的就不只是欣賞美感，還包括尊重不同的理解方式。久而久之，他們會明白，
很多事情其實不只得一種想法，而這種能力在日後的人生中會很有幫助。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say children benefit from learning about art because it nurtures both the heart and the mind. It helps them become more creative, more observant, and more well-rounded as people.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說孩子學習藝術之所以有益，是因為它同時滋養了他們的內心和思維。它能幫助他們成為更有創意、更善於觀察，也更全面發展的人。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say children benefit from learning about art
because it nurtures both the heart and the mind. It helps them become more
creative, more observant, and more well-rounded as people.
[總結來說，我會說孩子學習藝術之所以有益，是因為它同時滋養了他們的內心和思
維。它能幫助他們成為更有創意、更善於觀察，也更全面發展的人。]

question.english: Do you think the government should provide support for art and cultural activities?
question.chinese: 你認為政府應該支持藝術和文化活動嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think the government should provide support for art and
cultural activities?
你認為政府應該支持藝術和文化活動嗎？

components.english: Yes, I definitely think the government should provide support for art and cultural activities, because they are an important part of a society’s identity and quality of life.
components.chinese: 是的，我的確認為政府應該支持藝術和文化活動，因為它們是社會身份認同和生活質素中非常重要的一部分。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I definitely think the government should provide support for art
and cultural activities, because they are an important part of a society’s identity
and quality of life.
[是的，我的確認為政府應該支持藝術和文化活動，因為它們是社會身份認同和生活
質素中非常重要的一部分。]

components.english: To me, art and culture are not just decorative extras; they are part of the soul of a community. They preserve history, reflect shared values, and give people a stronger sense of belonging. On top of that, they make daily life richer and less mechanical. Without them, a city may still function, but it can start to feel flat and colourless. That is why I think the government has a role to play, especially because many valuable cultural activities cannot simply survive on commercial success alone.
components.chinese: 在我看來，藝術和文化並不是可有可無的點綴；它們其實是社群靈魂的一部分。它們保存歷史、反映共同價值，也讓人對自己身處的地方有更強的歸屬感。除此之外，它們也令日常生活變得更豐富，而不只是機械式地運作。如果沒有這些元素，一個城市雖然仍然可以照常運行，但很容易開始變得平面而失去色彩。這就是為甚麼我認為政府有責任扮演一定角色，尤其是因為很多有價值的文化活動，根本不能單靠商業收益來生存。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: To me, art and culture are not just decorative extras; they are
part of the soul of a community. They preserve history, reflect shared values,
and give people a stronger sense of belonging. On top of that, they make daily
life richer and less mechanical. Without them, a city may still function, but it
can start to feel flat and colourless. That is why I think the government has a
role to play, especially because many valuable cultural activities cannot simply
survive on commercial success alone.
[在我看來，藝術和文化並不是可有可無的點綴；它們其實是社群靈魂的一部分。它們
保存歷史、反映共同價值，也讓人對自己身處的地方有更強的歸屬感。除此之外，它們
也令日常生活變得更豐富，而不只是機械式地運作。如果沒有這些元素，一個城市雖
然仍然可以照常運行，但很容易開始變得平面而失去色彩。這就是為甚麼我認為政府
有責任扮演一定角色，尤其是因為很多有價值的文化活動，根本不能單靠商業收益來
生存。]

components.english: For example, local museums, theatre groups, heritage festivals, and public performances may not always make huge profits, but they can still have enormous cultural value. If the government helps fund them, more people can enjoy them, and traditional culture is less likely to fade into the background.
components.chinese: 例如，本地博物館、劇團、文化節慶，以及公共表演，未必一定能賺取可觀利潤，但它們仍然可能具有非常重大的文化價值。如果政府願意提供資助，便會有更多人能接觸和欣賞這些活動，而傳統文化也比較不容易慢慢被邊緣化。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, local museums, theatre groups, heritage festivals, and
public performances may not always make huge profits, but they can still have
enormous cultural value. If the government helps fund them, more people can
enjoy them, and traditional culture is less likely to fade into the background.
[例如，本地博物館、劇團、文化節慶，以及公共表演，未必一定能賺取可觀利潤，但它
們仍然可能具有非常重大的文化價值。如果政府願意提供資助，便會有更多人能接觸
和欣賞這些活動，而傳統文化也比較不容易慢慢被邊緣化。]

components.english: So yes, I would say government support is necessary, because art and culture help keep a society alive in more ways than one.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說政府的支持是有必要的，因為藝術和文化在不只一個層面上，都能讓一個社會保持活力。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say government support is necessary, because art
and culture help keep a society alive in more ways than one.
[所以，是的，我會說政府的支持是有必要的，因為藝術和文化在不只一個層面上，都
能讓一個社會保持活力。]

components.english: That said, I also think government support should be thoughtful and balanced, rather than simply throwing money at everything.
components.chinese: 不過，我也認為政府的支持應該是有思考、有平衡的，而不是把資源一股腦地灑向所有項目。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I also think government support should be thoughtful and
balanced, rather than simply throwing money at everything.
[不過，我也認為政府的支持應該是有思考、有平衡的，而不是把資源一股腦地灑向所
有項目。]

components.english: Public funding is important, but it should be used wisely. In my view, support should go not only to famous institutions, but also to community programmes, young artists, and traditional art forms that might otherwise be left to sink or swim. At the same time, there should be clear goals, fair selection, and public access, so that support does not become a closed circle for a privileged few. In other words, the government should act as a careful gardener, not just a careless spender.
components.chinese: 公共資助固然重要，但它必須被明智地運用。在我看來，支援不應該只流向知名機構，也應該惠及社區項目、年輕藝術家，以及那些原本很可能只能自生自滅的傳統藝術形式。與此同時，資助也應該有清晰目標、公平甄選，以及讓公眾能真正接觸到成果，這樣它才不會變成只屬於少數特權人士的小圈子。換句話說，政府應該像一個細心的園丁，而不只是胡亂花錢的人。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Public funding is important, but it should be used wisely. In
my view, support should go not only to famous institutions, but also to
community programmes, young artists, and traditional art forms that might
otherwise be left to sink or swim. At the same time, there should be clear goals,
fair selection, and public access, so that support does not become a closed circle
for a privileged few. In other words, the government should act as a careful
gardener, not just a careless spender.
[公共資助固然重要，但它必須被明智地運用。在我看來，支援不應該只流向知名機構
，也應該惠及社區項目、年輕藝術家，以及那些原本很可能只能自生自滅的傳統藝術
形式。與此同時，資助也應該有清晰目標、公平甄選，以及讓公眾能真正接觸到成果，
這樣它才不會變成只屬於少數特權人士的小圈子。換句話說，政府應該像一個細心的
園丁，而不只是胡亂花錢的人。]

components.english: For instance, instead of focusing all the money on a handful of major events, the government could also support school arts programmes, neighbourhood workshops, and small cultural festivals. That way, art would not remain behind velvet ropes; it would become something ordinary people can actually take part in.
components.chinese: 例如，政府與其把所有資源都集中在少數大型活動上，不如同時支持學校藝術課程、社區工作坊，以及小型文化節慶。那樣一來，藝術就不會只是被放在高高的位置、讓人遠觀而不可及；它會真正成為普通人也能參與其中的東西。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, instead of focusing all the money on a handful of
major events, the government could also support school arts programmes,
neighbourhood workshops, and small cultural festivals. That way, art would not
remain behind velvet ropes; it would become something ordinary people can
actually take part in.
[例如，政府與其把所有資源都集中在少數大型活動上，不如同時支持學校藝術課程、
社區工作坊，以及小型文化節慶。那樣一來，藝術就不會只是被放在高高的位置、讓人
遠觀而不可及；它會真正成為普通人也能參與其中的東西。]

components.english: Overall, I believe the government should absolutely support art and cultural activities, but the support needs to be fair, strategic, and accessible to the wider public.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我絕對認為政府應該支持藝術和文化活動，但這種支持必須是公平的、有策略的，而且能真正讓更廣泛的公眾受惠。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I believe the government should absolutely support art
and cultural activities, but the support needs to be fair, strategic, and accessible
to the wider public.
[總結來說，我絕對認為政府應該支持藝術和文化活動，但這種支持必須是公平的、有
策略的，而且能真正讓更廣泛的公眾受惠。]

question.english: How could art exhibitions attract more visitors?
question.chinese: 藝術展覽可以怎樣吸引更多參觀者？

source_lines.text: Topic: How could art exhibitions attract more visitors?
藝術展覽可以怎樣吸引更多參觀者？

components.english: I think one of the best ways for art exhibitions to attract more visitors is to make the experience more engaging and interactive.
components.chinese: 我認為，藝術展覽要吸引更多參觀者，其中一個最好的方法就是令整個體驗變得更有趣、更有互動性。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one of the best ways for art exhibitions to attract more visitors is
to make the experience more engaging and interactive.
[我認為，藝術展覽要吸引更多參觀者，其中一個最好的方法就是令整個體驗變得更
有趣、更有互動性。]

components.english: Sometimes people stay away from exhibitions because they assume they will be too quiet, too formal, or even a little intimidating. If that happens, it becomes an uphill battle to get people through the door. By contrast, if exhibitions include guided tours, interactive displays, multimedia elements, or simple explanations that bring the artwork to life, visitors are far more likely to feel connected. In other words, people are more easily drawn in when art feels alive rather than distant.
components.chinese: 有時候，人們不去看展覽，是因為他們先入為主地覺得展覽太安靜、太正式，甚至有點令人卻步。如果是這樣，要把人吸引進場就會變得相當困難。相反，如果展覽加入導賞、互動裝置、多媒體元素，或者一些能讓作品變得更容易理解的簡單說明，參觀者就會更容易產生連結。換句話說，當藝術給人的感覺是活生生的，而不是遙不可及的，人們自然更容易被吸引。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Sometimes people stay away from exhibitions because they
assume they will be too quiet, too formal, or even a little intimidating. If that
happens, it becomes an uphill battle to get people through the door. By contrast,
if exhibitions include guided tours, interactive displays, multimedia elements, or
simple explanations that bring the artwork to life, visitors are far more likely to
feel connected. In other words, people are more easily drawn in when art feels
alive rather than distant.
[有時候，人們不去看展覽，是因為他們先入為主地覺得展覽太安靜、太正式，甚至有
點令人卻步。如果是這樣，要把人吸引進場就會變得相當困難。相反，如果展覽加入導
賞、互動裝置、多媒體元素，或者一些能讓作品變得更容易理解的簡單說明，參觀者就
會更容易產生連結。換句話說，當藝術給人的感覺是活生生的，而不是遙不可及的，人
們自然更容易被吸引。]

components.english: For example, instead of simply hanging paintings on a wall, an exhibition could include short videos, audio guides, hands-on workshops, or spaces where visitors can create something of their own. That way, the exhibition becomes more than something people just look at; it becomes something they step into.
components.chinese: 例如，一個展覽與其只是把畫掛在牆上，不如加入短片、語音導覽、親身參與的工作坊，或者讓參觀者自己動手創作的小空間。那樣一來，展覽就不再只是讓人「看」的東西，而是變成一個讓人真正走進去體驗的世界。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, instead of simply hanging paintings on a wall, an
exhibition could include short videos, audio guides, hands-on workshops, or
spaces where visitors can create something of their own. That way, the
exhibition becomes more than something people just look at; it becomes
something they step into.
[例如，一個展覽與其只是把畫掛在牆上，不如加入短片、語音導覽、親身參與的工作
坊，或者讓參觀者自己動手創作的小空間。那樣一來，展覽就不再只是讓人「看」的東
西，而是變成一個讓人真正走進去體驗的世界。]

components.english: So, one key way to attract more visitors is to make exhibitions more vivid, approachable, and memorable.
components.chinese: 所以，要吸引更多參觀者，其中一個關鍵方法就是令展覽變得更生動、更容易親近，也更令人難忘。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one key way to attract more visitors is to make exhibitions
more vivid, approachable, and memorable.
[所以，要吸引更多參觀者，其中一個關鍵方法就是令展覽變得更生動、更容易親近，
也更令人難忘。]

components.english: Another important way is to make exhibitions more accessible, both in terms of promotion and in terms of everyday convenience.
components.chinese: 另一個重要的方法，就是令展覽在宣傳和日常便利性上都變得更容易接觸。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another important way is to make exhibitions more accessible, both in
terms of promotion and in terms of everyday convenience.
[另一個重要的方法，就是令展覽在宣傳和日常便利性上都變得更容易接觸。]

components.english: Even a brilliant exhibition can remain under the radar if people do not hear about it, or if visiting feels inconvenient. That is why organisers need to promote exhibitions well through social media, schools, community groups, and online platforms. At the same time, they should think about ticket prices, opening hours, location, and whether the event feels welcoming to families, students, and ordinary members of the public. To me, art exhibitions should open doors, not put up invisible walls.
components.chinese: 即使一個展覽本身非常出色，如果人們根本不知道它的存在，或者覺得去參觀很不方便，它仍然可能默默無聞。這就是為甚麼主辦方需要透過社交媒體、學校、社區團體，以及網上平台好好宣傳展覽。與此同時，他們也應該考慮票價、開放時間、地點，以及活動是否對家庭、學生和普通市民都夠友善。在我看來，藝術展覽應該是打開大門，而不是設下看不見的高牆。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Even a brilliant exhibition can remain under the radar if
people do not hear about it, or if visiting feels inconvenient. That is why
organisers need to promote exhibitions well through social media, schools,
community groups, and online platforms. At the same time, they should think
about ticket prices, opening hours, location, and whether the event feels
welcoming to families, students, and ordinary members of the public. To me, art
exhibitions should open doors, not put up invisible walls.
[即使一個展覽本身非常出色，如果人們根本不知道它的存在，或者覺得去參觀很不
方便，它仍然可能默默無聞。這就是為甚麼主辦方需要透過社交媒體、學校、社區團體
，以及網上平台好好宣傳展覽。與此同時，他們也應該考慮票價、開放時間、地點，以
及活動是否對家庭、學生和普通市民都夠友善。在我看來，藝術展覽應該是打開大門，
而不是設下看不見的高牆。]

components.english: For instance, if an exhibition offers discounted student tickets, weekend activities, and eye-catching promotion online, it is much more likely to attract young people and first-time visitors. In the same way, if schools organise visits or local communities are invited to take part, the exhibition can reach people who might never have set foot in a gallery otherwise.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個展覽提供學生優惠票、週末活動，以及在網上做出吸引眼球的宣傳，它就更有機會吸引年輕人和第一次來看展覽的人。同樣地，如果學校安排參觀，或者邀請本地社區一起參與，展覽就能接觸到那些原本可能從來不會踏進畫廊的人。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if an exhibition offers discounted student tickets,
weekend activities, and eye-catching promotion online, it is much more likely to
attract young people and first-time visitors. In the same way, if schools organise
visits or local communities are invited to take part, the exhibition can reach
people who might never have set foot in a gallery otherwise.
[例如，如果一個展覽提供學生優惠票、週末活動，以及在網上做出吸引眼球的宣傳，
它就更有機會吸引年輕人和第一次來看展覽的人。同樣地，如果學校安排參觀，或者
邀請本地社區一起參與，展覽就能接觸到那些原本可能從來不會踏進畫廊的人。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say art exhibitions could attract more visitors by becoming both more engaging and more accessible. If organisers can strike that balance, art will feel less like a closed world and more like something everyone can enjoy.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說藝術展覽要吸引更多參觀者，就要同時做到更有吸引力，也更容易接觸。如果主辦方能夠在這兩方面取得平衡，藝術就不會再像一個封閉的世界，而會更像是人人都能欣賞的東西。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say art exhibitions could attract more visitors by
becoming both more engaging and more accessible. If organisers can strike that
balance, art will feel less like a closed world and more like something everyone
can enjoy.
[總結來說，我會說藝術展覽要吸引更多參觀者，就要同時做到更有吸引力，也更容易
接觸。如果主辦方能夠在這兩方面取得平衡，藝術就不會再像一個封閉的世界，而會
更像是人人都能欣賞的東西。]

question.english: Are art exhibitions popular in your country?
question.chinese: 你國家的藝術展覽受歡迎嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Are art exhibitions popular in your country?
你國家的藝術展覽受歡迎嗎？

components.english: In Hong Kong, I’d say art exhibitions are fairly popular, especially among younger people, tourists, and those who already have an interest in culture and design.
components.chinese: 在香港，我會說藝術展覽算是相當受歡迎，特別是在年輕人、遊客，以及本身對文化和設計有興趣的人之中。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In Hong Kong, I’d say art exhibitions are fairly popular, especially
among younger people, tourists, and those who already have an interest in
culture and design.
[在香港，我會說藝術展覽算是相當受歡迎，特別是在年輕人、遊客，以及本身對文化
和設計有興趣的人之中。]

components.english: Hong Kong has built a much more visible arts scene over the years, with major venues such as M+, the Hong Kong Museum of Art, Tai Kwun, and the wider West Kowloon Cultural District all helping to bring exhibitions into the public eye. On top of that, high-profile events like Art Basel Hong Kong and Tai Kwun Art Week also show that exhibitions are not just a niche interest; they are part of the city’s broader cultural life. In a way, art exhibitions have become one of the ways Hong Kong shows its cultural pulse.
components.chinese: 多年以來，香港已經建立起一個更容易被看見的藝術文化環境，像 M+、香港藝術館、大館，以及整個西九文化區這些重要場地，都令藝術展覽更進一步走進公眾視野。除此之外，像香港巴塞爾藝術展和大館藝術週這類具知名度的活動，也反映出展覽並不只是小眾興趣，而是城市整體文化生活的一部分。某程度上，藝術展覽已經成為香港展現文化脈搏的其中一種方式。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Hong Kong has built a much more visible arts scene over the
years, with major venues such as M+, the Hong Kong Museum of Art, Tai
Kwun, and the wider West Kowloon Cultural District all helping to bring
exhibitions into the public eye. On top of that, high-profile events like Art Basel
Hong Kong and Tai Kwun Art Week also show that exhibitions are not just a
niche interest; they are part of the city’s broader cultural life. In a way, art
exhibitions have become one of the ways Hong Kong shows its cultural pulse.
[多年以來，香港已經建立起一個更容易被看見的藝術文化環境，像 M+、香港藝術
館、大館，以及整個西九文化區這些重要場地，都令藝術展覽更進一步走進公眾視野。
除此之外，像香港巴塞爾藝術展和大館藝術週這類具知名度的活動，也反映出展覽並
不只是小眾興趣，而是城市整體文化生活的一部分。某程度上，藝術展覽已經成為香
港展現文化脈搏的其中一種方式。]

components.english: For example, if there is a major exhibition at M+ or a well-promoted event during Hong Kong’s art season, it can generate a lot of attention quite quickly. People may go because they genuinely love art, but others may simply be curious, want a cultural day out, or enjoy taking in something new with friends or family.
components.chinese: 例如，如果 M+ 有大型展覽，或者在香港藝術季期間有宣傳做得很好的活動，往往很快就能吸引大量關注。有些人去看展覽，當然是因為他們本身真的喜歡藝術；但也有些人只是出於好奇，想過一個有文化氣息的假日，或者和朋友家人一起看看新鮮事物。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if there is a major exhibition at M+ or a
well-promoted event during Hong Kong’s art season, it can generate a lot of
attention quite quickly. People may go because they genuinely love art, but
others may simply be curious, want a cultural day out, or enjoy taking in
something new with friends or family.
[例如，如果 M+ 有大型展覽，或者在香港藝術季期間有宣傳做得很好的活動，往往很
快就能吸引大量關注。有些人去看展覽，當然是因為他們本身真的喜歡藝術；但也有
些人只是出於好奇，想過一個有文化氣息的假日，或者和朋友家人一起看看新鮮事

components.english: So yes, overall I would say art exhibitions are popular in Hong Kong, particularly when they are accessible, well-curated, and tied to major cultural venues.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，整體來說我會說藝術展覽在香港是受歡迎的，特別是當它們容易接觸、策展出色，而且和重要文化場地連結在一起的時候。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, overall I would say art exhibitions are popular in Hong
Kong, particularly when they are accessible, well-curated, and tied to major
cultural venues.
[所以，是的，整體來說我會說藝術展覽在香港是受歡迎的，特別是當它們容易接觸、
策展出色，而且和重要文化場地連結在一起的時候。]

components.english: That said, I do not think art exhibitions are equally popular with everyone, because Hong Kong is also a very fast-paced and practical city.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為藝術展覽對每一類人都同樣受歡迎，因為香港同時也是一個節奏很快、很講求實際的城市。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think art exhibitions are equally popular with
everyone, because Hong Kong is also a very fast-paced and practical city.
[不過，我並不認為藝術展覽對每一類人都同樣受歡迎，因為香港同時也是一個節奏
很快、很講求實際的城市。]

components.english: In everyday life, many people are busy with work, study, and financial pressure, so exhibitions may not always be at the top of their list. Some people still see art as something interesting but not essential. So, while exhibitions can attract strong public interest, especially when they are high-profile or visually engaging, they do not necessarily become part of everyone’s regular routine. In other words, they are popular, but not always front and centre for the average person.
components.chinese: 在日常生活中，很多人都忙於工作、讀書，以及各種經濟壓力，所以展覽未必一定會排在他們生活清單的最前面。有些人仍然會把藝術看成有趣，但不是必需的東西。所以，雖然展覽可以吸引相當大的公眾興趣，特別是當它們規模大或者視覺效果強時，它們也未必會成為每個人固定的生活習慣。換句話說，它們是受歡迎的，但未必總是普通人生活中最核心的部分。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: In everyday life, many people are busy with work, study, and
financial pressure, so exhibitions may not always be at the top of their list. Some
people still see art as something interesting but not essential. So, while
exhibitions can attract strong public interest, especially when they are
high-profile or visually engaging, they do not necessarily become part of
everyone’s regular routine. In other words, they are popular, but not always
front and centre for the average person.
[在日常生活中，很多人都忙於工作、讀書，以及各種經濟壓力，所以展覽未必一定會排在
他們生活清單的最前面。有些人仍然會把藝術看成有趣，但不是必需的東西。所以，雖然展
覽可以吸引相當大的公眾興趣，特別是當它們規模大或者視覺效果強時，它們也未必會成
為每個人固定的生活習慣。換句話說，它們是受歡迎的，但未必總是普通人生活中最核心的
部分。]

components.english: For instance, a blockbuster show, a digital installation, or an exhibition connected to a famous museum is likely to draw bigger crowds than a quieter, more academic exhibition. That suggests popularity in Hong Kong is real, but it often depends on how approachable and well-publicised the exhibition is.
components.chinese: 例如，一個大型展覽、數碼裝置展，或者和著名博物館有關的展覽，通常會比一個較安靜、較學術性的展覽吸引更多人。這說明香港對展覽的受歡迎程度是真實存在的，只不過它往往取決於那個展覽是否夠容易親近，以及宣傳是否做得好。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a blockbuster show, a digital installation, or an
exhibition connected to a famous museum is likely to draw bigger crowds than a
quieter, more academic exhibition. That suggests popularity in Hong Kong is
real, but it often depends on how approachable and well-publicised the
exhibition is.
[例如，一個大型展覽、數碼裝置展，或者和著名博物館有關的展覽，通常會比一個較安靜、
較學術性的展覽吸引更多人。這說明香港對展覽的受歡迎程度是真實存在的，只不過它往
往取決於那個展覽是否夠容易親近，以及宣傳是否做得好。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say art exhibitions are definitely popular in Hong Kong, but their appeal is strongest when they feel fresh, accessible, and relevant to ordinary people rather than only to serious art lovers.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說藝術展覽在香港的確是受歡迎的，只不過當它們給人的感覺夠新鮮、夠容易接觸，而且和普通人的生活有連結時，它們的吸引力往往會最強，而不只是停留在嚴肅藝術愛好者之中。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say art exhibitions are definitely popular in Hong
Kong, but their appeal is strongest when they feel fresh, accessible, and relevant
to ordinary people rather than only to serious art lovers.
[總結來說，我會說藝術展覽在香港的確是受歡迎的，只不過當它們給人的感覺夠新
鮮、夠容易接觸，而且和普通人的生活有連結時，它們的吸引力往往會最強，而不只是
停留在嚴肅藝術愛好者之中。]

question.english: Do you think people should have to pay to visit art exhibitions?
question.chinese: 你認為人們參觀藝術展覽是否應該付費？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think people should have to pay to visit art exhibitions?
你認為人們參觀藝術展覽是否應該付費？

components.english: Personally, I think people should sometimes have to pay to visit art exhibitions, especially when the exhibition is large, special, or expensive to organise.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我認為人們在某些情況下應該為參觀藝術展覽付費，特別是當展覽規模很大、很特別，或者籌辦成本很高的時候。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I think people should sometimes have to pay to visit art
exhibitions, especially when the exhibition is large, special, or expensive to
organise.
[就我個人而言，我認為人們在某些情況下應該為參觀藝術展覽付費，特別是當展覽
規模很大、很特別，或者籌辦成本很高的時候。]

components.english: The main reason is that exhibitions do not appear out of thin air. They usually involve venue costs, transport, insurance, lighting, security, curation, and promotion. If everything were free all the time, it could be much harder for museums and galleries to keep the lights on and maintain a high standard. In that sense, paying for a ticket can be seen as a fair contribution rather than an unfair burden.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，展覽並不是憑空出現的。它通常牽涉場地成本、運輸、保險、燈光、保安、策展，以及宣傳等各方面開支。如果所有展覽永遠都是免費的，那麼博物館和畫廊要維持運作和保持高水準，就可能會困難得多。從這個角度來看，支付門票更像是一種合理的分擔，而不是不公平的負擔。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that exhibitions do not appear out of thin
air. They usually involve venue costs, transport, insurance, lighting, security,
curation, and promotion. If everything were free all the time, it could be much
harder for museums and galleries to keep the lights on and maintain a high
standard. In that sense, paying for a ticket can be seen as a fair contribution
rather than an unfair burden.
[主要原因是，展覽並不是憑空出現的。它通常牽涉場地成本、運輸、保險、燈光、保
安、策展，以及宣傳等各方面開支。如果所有展覽永遠都是免費的，那麼博物館和畫廊
要維持運作和保持高水準，就可能會困難得多。從這個角度來看，支付門票更像是一
種合理的分擔，而不是不公平的負擔。]

components.english: For example, if a museum brings in rare works from overseas or creates a highly immersive exhibition with digital installations, it seems reasonable to charge an entry fee. After all, experiences like that do not come cheap, so it makes sense for visitors to pay at least part of the cost.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一間博物館從海外借來珍貴作品，或者打造一個有數碼裝置的沉浸式展覽，那麼收取入場費其實是合理的。畢竟，這類體驗本身成本不低，所以由參觀者分擔部分費用，也是合情合理的。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a museum brings in rare works from overseas or
creates a highly immersive exhibition with digital installations, it seems
reasonable to charge an entry fee. After all, experiences like that do not come
cheap, so it makes sense for visitors to pay at least part of the cost.
[例如，如果一間博物館從海外借來珍貴作品，或者打造一個有數碼裝置的沉浸式展
覽，那麼收取入場費其實是合理的。畢竟，這類體驗本身成本不低，所以由參觀者分擔
部分費用，也是合情合理的。]

components.english: So yes, I do think charging for art exhibitions can be justified, especially when the fee helps support quality and sustainability.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我的確認為藝術展覽收費是可以被合理化的，特別是當這筆費用有助於支持展覽質素和長遠運作的時候。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I do think charging for art exhibitions can be justified,
especially when the fee helps support quality and sustainability.
[所以，是的，我的確認為藝術展覽收費是可以被合理化的，特別是當這筆費用有助於
支持展覽質素和長遠運作的時候。]

components.english: That said, I do not think art should be priced so highly that ordinary people are shut out.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為藝術應該被定價得那麼高，以致普通人都被拒諸門外。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think art should be priced so highly that ordinary
people are shut out.
[不過，我並不認為藝術應該被定價得那麼高，以致普通人都被拒諸門外。]

components.english: To me, art is not just a luxury for the well-off; it is part of public culture and shared education. If ticket prices are too high, exhibitions may end up preaching to the converted and losing the wider public. That would defeat the purpose. So, while some payment is fair, there should still be affordable tickets, student discounts, or even free admission on certain days. In other words, exhibitions need to strike a balance between staying afloat and opening their doors.
components.chinese: 在我看來，藝術不應該只是富裕人士的奢侈享受；它其實也是公共文化和共同教育的一部分。如果票價太高，展覽最後可能只會吸引本來就有興趣的人，而失去更廣泛的公眾，那就有點本末倒置了。所以，雖然適度收費是合理的，但仍然應該有可負擔的票價、學生優惠，甚至某些日子的免費入場安排。換句話說，展覽需要在維持營運和向公眾打開大門之間取得平衡。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To me, art is not just a luxury for the well-off; it is part of
public culture and shared education. If ticket prices are too high, exhibitions
may end up preaching to the converted and losing the wider public. That would
defeat the purpose. So, while some payment is fair, there should still be
affordable tickets, student discounts, or even free admission on certain days. In
other words, exhibitions need to strike a balance between staying afloat and
opening their doors.
[在我看來，藝術不應該只是富裕人士的奢侈享受；它其實也是公共文化和共同教育的
一部分。如果票價太高，展覽最後可能只會吸引本來就有興趣的人，而失去更廣泛的
公眾，那就有點本末倒置了。所以，雖然適度收費是合理的，但仍然應該有可負擔的票
價、學生優惠，甚至某些日子的免費入場安排。換句話說，展覽需要在維持營運和向公
眾打開大門之間取得平衡。]

components.english: For instance, a gallery could charge full price for a major international exhibition, but offer reduced tickets for students, senior citizens, and families. It could also provide free community days now and then. That way, art would not be locked behind a velvet rope.
components.chinese: 例如，一間畫廊可以為大型國際展覽收取正常票價，但同時為學生、長者和家庭提供優惠門票，並且不時設立社區免費開放日。那樣一來，藝術就不會像被一條天鵝絨繩索隔開，只留給少數人欣賞。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a gallery could charge full price for a major
international exhibition, but offer reduced tickets for students, senior citizens,
and families. It could also provide free community days now and then. That
way, art would not be locked behind a velvet rope.
[例如，一間畫廊可以為大型國際展覽收取正常票價，但同時為學生、長者和家庭提供
優惠門票，並且不時設立社區免費開放日。那樣一來，藝術就不會像被一條天鵝絨繩
索隔開，只留給少數人欣賞。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people can be asked to pay for art exhibitions, but the cost should remain fair and accessible. Art should have value, of course, but it should not be priced out of people’s reach.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說人們可以為參觀藝術展覽付費，但費用應該保持公平而且容易負擔。藝術當然應該被珍惜，也應該有其價值，但它不應該因為價格而變得遙不可及。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people can be asked to pay for art exhibitions,
but the cost should remain fair and accessible. Art should have value, of course,
but it should not be priced out of people’s reach.
[總結來說，我會說人們可以為參觀藝術展覽付費，但費用應該保持公平而且容易負
擔。藝術當然應該被珍惜，也應該有其價值，但它不應該因為價格而變得遙不可及。]

question.english: How will art exhibitions change in the future?
question.chinese: 未來藝術展覽會怎樣改變？

source_lines.text: Topic: How will art exhibitions change in the future?
未來藝術展覽會怎樣改變？

components.english: I think art exhibitions will become much more immersive and interactive in the future.
components.chinese: 我認為，未來的藝術展覽會變得更加沉浸式，也更加講求互動。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think art exhibitions will become much more immersive and
interactive in the future.
[我認為，未來的藝術展覽會變得更加沉浸式，也更加講求互動。]

components.english: In the past, exhibitions were often quite traditional, in the sense that people simply walked around and looked at artworks on the wall. In the future, I think that model will gradually evolve. With digital technology, sound, projection, and interactive design, exhibitions are likely to become experiences that people step into rather than just observe from a distance. In other words, art will not just hang there in silence; it will start to speak more directly to the audience.
components.chinese: 過去的展覽通常比較傳統，某程度上來說，人們只是走來走去，看牆上的作品而已。但在未來，我認為這種模式會慢慢演變。隨著數碼科技、聲音、投影，以及互動設計的發展，展覽很可能會變成一種讓人走進去體驗的經歷，而不再只是從遠處觀看。換句話說，藝術不再只是安靜地掛在那裡；它會開始更直接地和觀眾說話。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: In the past, exhibitions were often quite traditional, in the sense
that people simply walked around and looked at artworks on the wall. In the
future, I think that model will gradually evolve. With digital technology, sound,
projection, and interactive design, exhibitions are likely to become experiences
that people step into rather than just observe from a distance. In other words,
art will not just hang there in silence; it will start to speak more directly to the
audience.
[過去的展覽通常比較傳統，某程度上來說，人們只是走來走去，看牆上的作品而已。
但在未來，我認為這種模式會慢慢演變。隨著數碼科技、聲音、投影，以及互動設計的
發展，展覽很可能會變成一種讓人走進去體驗的經歷，而不再只是從遠處觀看。換句
話說，藝術不再只是安靜地掛在那裡；它會開始更直接地和觀眾說話。]

components.english: For example, instead of reading a long label beside a painting, visitors may use augmented reality or audio guides to see the story behind the artwork unfold in front of them. In the same way, an exhibition may include moving images, music, or interactive spaces that allow visitors to engage with the work rather than just glance at it and move on.
components.chinese: 例如，參觀者未來可能不再只是站在一幅畫旁邊讀一大段文字，而是透過擴增實境或語音導覽，直接看見作品背後的故事在眼前展開。同樣地，一個展覽也可能加入動態影像、音樂，或者互動空間，讓參觀者真正和作品產生互動，而不是匆匆看一眼就離開。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, instead of reading a long label beside a painting,
visitors may use augmented reality or audio guides to see the story behind the
artwork unfold in front of them. In the same way, an exhibition may include
moving images, music, or interactive spaces that allow visitors to engage with
the work rather than just glance at it and move on.
[例如，參觀者未來可能不再只是站在一幅畫旁邊讀一大段文字，而是透過擴增實境
或語音導覽，直接看見作品背後的故事在眼前展開。同樣地，一個展覽也可能加入動
態影像、音樂，或者互動空間，讓參觀者真正和作品產生互動，而不是匆匆看一眼就離

components.english: So, one major change is that art exhibitions will probably become more vivid, multi-sensory, and engaging than they are now.
components.chinese: 所以，其中一個主要變化就是，未來的藝術展覽很可能會比現在更加生動、多感官，也更能吸引人投入。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major change is that art exhibitions will probably become
more vivid, multi-sensory, and engaging than they are now.
[所以，其中一個主要變化就是，未來的藝術展覽很可能會比現在更加生動、多感官，
也更能吸引人投入。]

components.english: Another change is that art exhibitions will probably become more accessible and more flexible, both physically and digitally.
components.chinese: 另一個變化是，藝術展覽未來大概會在實體和數碼層面上都變得更容易接觸，也更靈活。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another change is that art exhibitions will probably become more
accessible and more flexible, both physically and digitally.
[另一個變化是，藝術展覽未來大概會在實體和數碼層面上都變得更容易接觸，也更
靈活。]

components.english: I think exhibitions will increasingly try to reach wider audiences rather than speaking only to serious art lovers. That means more online tours, more multilingual content, more educational programmes, and more efforts to make visitors feel welcome instead of intimidated. At the same time, I do not think physical exhibitions will disappear into thin air. A screen can open the door, but standing in front of an original artwork still has a different kind of magic. So, to me, the future is likely to be hybrid: more digital, but not purely digital.
components.chinese: 我認為，未來的展覽會越來越努力接觸更廣泛的觀眾，而不只是對嚴肅的藝術愛好者說話。這意味著會有更多線上導覽、多語言內容、教育活動，以及更多讓參觀者感到被歡迎、而不是被嚇退的安排。與此同時，我並不認為實體展覽會憑空消失。螢幕也許可以替人打開一道門，但真正站在原作面前，仍然有另一種無可取代的魅力。所以，在我看來，未來很可能會是混合式的：更數碼化，但不會完全數碼化。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: I think exhibitions will increasingly try to reach wider
audiences rather than speaking only to serious art lovers. That means more
online tours, more multilingual content, more educational programmes, and
more efforts to make visitors feel welcome instead of intimidated. At the same
time, I do not think physical exhibitions will disappear into thin air. A screen
can open the door, but standing in front of an original artwork still has a
different kind of magic. So, to me, the future is likely to be hybrid: more digital,
but not purely digital.
[我認為，未來的展覽會越來越努力接觸更廣泛的觀眾，而不只是對嚴肅的藝術愛好
者說話。這意味著會有更多線上導覽、多語言內容、教育活動，以及更多讓參觀者感到
被歡迎、而不是被嚇退的安排。與此同時，我並不認為實體展覽會憑空消失。螢幕也許
可以替人打開一道門，但真正站在原作面前，仍然有另一種無可取代的魅力。所以，在
我看來，未來很可能會是混合式的：更數碼化，但不會完全數碼化。]

components.english: For instance, a museum may allow people to preview an exhibition online, listen to background stories on an app, and then visit the physical venue for the full experience. That way, the exhibition can meet people halfway and attract both casual visitors and devoted art fans.
components.chinese: 例如，一間博物館未來可能會讓人先在線上預覽展覽、透過應用程式聽背景故事，然後再親身到場感受完整體驗。這樣一來，展覽就能主動走近觀眾，同時吸引隨意參觀的人和真正熱愛藝術的人。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a museum may allow people to preview an exhibition
online, listen to background stories on an app, and then visit the physical venue
for the full experience. That way, the exhibition can meet people halfway and
attract both casual visitors and devoted art fans.
[例如，一間博物館未來可能會讓人先在線上預覽展覽、透過應用程式聽背景故事，然
後再親身到場感受完整體驗。這樣一來，展覽就能主動走近觀眾，同時吸引隨意參觀
的人和真正熱愛藝術的人。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say art exhibitions in the future will become more interactive, more inclusive, and more technology-driven. That said, however modern they become, their real purpose will still be the same: to help people connect with art in a meaningful way.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說未來的藝術展覽將會變得更互動、更具包容性，也更依賴科技。不過，不管它們變得多現代，真正的核心目的仍然不會改變，那就是幫助人們以有意義的方式與藝術建立連結。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say art exhibitions in the future will become more
interactive, more inclusive, and more technology-driven. That said, however
modern they become, their real purpose will still be the same: to help people
connect with art in a meaningful way.
[總結來說，我會說未來的藝術展覽將會變得更互動、更具包容性，也更依賴科技。不
過，不管它們變得多現代，真正的核心目的仍然不會改變，那就是幫助人們以有意義
的方式與藝術建立連結。]

question.english: What can you do to support an artist?
question.chinese: 你可以做些甚麼去支持一位藝術家？

source_lines.text: Topic: What can you do to support an artist?
你可以做些甚麼去支持一位藝術家？

components.english: I think the most direct way to support an artist is to value their work properly, whether by buying it, paying for a ticket, or using legal paid platforms.
components.chinese: 我認為，支持一位藝術家最直接的方法，就是真正重視他們的作品，例如購買作品、付費入場，或者使用合法的付費平台。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think the most direct way to support an artist is to value their work
properly, whether by buying it, paying for a ticket, or using legal paid
platforms.
[我認為，支持一位藝術家最直接的方法，就是真正重視他們的作品，例如購買作品、
付費入場，或者使用合法的付費平台。]

components.english: A lot of people say they admire art, but admiration alone does not pay the bills. Artists need time, training, materials, and financial stability in order to keep creating. If people always expect music, paintings, performances, or design work to be free, artists can easily end up fighting an uphill battle. In that sense, paying for creative work is not just a transaction; it is a concrete sign of respect.
components.chinese: 很多人都說自己欣賞藝術，但單靠欣賞本身並不能幫人交租。藝術家要持續創作，其實需要時間、訓練、材料，以及一定的經濟穩定。如果人們總是覺得音樂、畫作、表演，或者設計作品都應該免費，那麼藝術家就很容易陷入一場艱難的苦戰。從這個角度來說，為創作付費不只是一種交易，更是一種很具體的尊重。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: A lot of people say they admire art, but admiration alone does
not pay the bills. Artists need time, training, materials, and financial stability in
order to keep creating. If people always expect music, paintings, performances,
or design work to be free, artists can easily end up fighting an uphill battle. In
that sense, paying for creative work is not just a transaction; it is a concrete sign
of respect.
[很多人都說自己欣賞藝術，但單靠欣賞本身並不能幫人交租。藝術家要持續創作，其
實需要時間、訓練、材料，以及一定的經濟穩定。如果人們總是覺得音樂、畫作、表演，
或者設計作品都應該免費，那麼藝術家就很容易陷入一場艱難的苦戰。從這個角度來
說，為創作付費不只是一種交易，更是一種很具體的尊重。]

components.english: For example, if I enjoy a local singer’s music, I can buy a concert ticket, stream their songs legally, or even purchase an album or some merchandise. Those choices may seem small, but little by little they can help keep an artist afloat.
components.chinese: 例如，如果我喜歡一位本地歌手的音樂，我可以買演唱會門票、透過合法平台聽歌，甚至購買專輯或周邊產品。這些選擇看起來也許很小，但一點一滴累積起來，其實能幫助藝術家維持下去。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if I enjoy a local singer’s music, I can buy a concert
ticket, stream their songs legally, or even purchase an album or some
merchandise. Those choices may seem small, but little by little they can help
keep an artist afloat.
[例如，如果我喜歡一位本地歌手的音樂，我可以買演唱會門票、透過合法平台聽歌，
甚至購買專輯或周邊產品。這些選擇看起來也許很小，但一點一滴累積起來，其實能
幫助藝術家維持下去。]

components.english: So, one practical way to support an artist is to put your money where your mouth is and pay for the work you genuinely value.
components.chinese: 所以，支持藝術家其中一個最實際的方法，就是用行動而不只是用嘴巴去表達支持，並且願意為你真正欣賞的作品付費。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one practical way to support an artist is to put your money
where your mouth is and pay for the work you genuinely value.
[所以，支持藝術家其中一個最實際的方法，就是用行動而不只是用嘴巴去表達支持，
並且願意為你真正欣賞的作品付費。]

components.english: Another important way to support an artist is to help more people discover their work.
components.chinese: 另一個重要的支持方法，就是幫助更多人看見他們的作品。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another important way to support an artist is to help more people
discover their work.
[另一個重要的支持方法，就是幫助更多人看見他們的作品。]

components.english: In creative fields, word of mouth can go a very long way. Sharing an artist’s work, recommending them to friends, following their pages, or simply showing up at exhibitions and performances can all make a real difference. Artists often need not only money, but also visibility, encouragement, and a sense that their work is reaching people. Sometimes, a bit of genuine support can be the wind beneath their wings.
components.chinese: 在創意領域裡，口碑的力量往往可以走得很遠。分享藝術家的作品、推薦給朋友、追蹤他們的專頁，或者單純去看展覽和表演，其實都能帶來真正的幫助。藝術家需要的不只是金錢，還包括曝光、鼓勵，以及一種「自己的作品真的被人看見」的感覺。有時候，一點真誠的支持，就足以成為推動他們前進的力量。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: In creative fields, word of mouth can go a very long way.
Sharing an artist’s work, recommending them to friends, following their pages,
or simply showing up at exhibitions and performances can all make a real
difference. Artists often need not only money, but also visibility,
encouragement, and a sense that their work is reaching people. Sometimes, a bit
of genuine support can be the wind beneath their wings.
[在創意領域裡，口碑的力量往往可以走得很遠。分享藝術家的作品、推薦給朋友、追
蹤他們的專頁，或者單純去看展覽和表演，其實都能帶來真正的幫助。藝術家需要的
不只是金錢，還包括曝光、鼓勵，以及一種「自己的作品真的被人看見」的感覺。有時候
，一點真誠的支持，就足以成為推動他們前進的力量。]

components.english: For instance, if I know a young illustrator or photographer whose work is impressive, I can repost it online, tell friends about it, or attend their event instead of just scrolling past. That kind of support may open doors that would otherwise stay shut.
components.chinese: 例如，如果我認識一位作品很出色的年輕插畫家或攝影師，我可以把作品分享到網上、介紹給朋友，或者親身去支持他們的活動，而不是看過就算。這種支持有時甚至能打開原本不會被打開的門。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if I know a young illustrator or photographer whose
work is impressive, I can repost it online, tell friends about it, or attend their
event instead of just scrolling past. That kind of support may open doors that
would otherwise stay shut.
[例如，如果我認識一位作品很出色的年輕插畫家或攝影師，我可以把作品分享到網
上、介紹給朋友，或者親身去支持他們的活動，而不是看過就算。這種支持有時甚至能
打開原本不會被打開的門。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the best way to support an artist is to combine practical support with genuine appreciation. If people are willing to pay for the work, talk about it, and show up for it, artists are far more likely to keep creating and thrive.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說支持藝術家最好的方法，就是把實際支持和真誠欣賞結合起來。如果人們願意為作品付費、願意主動談論它，也願意親身去支持它，藝術家就更有可能持續創作，並且發展得更好。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say the best way to support an artist is to combine
practical support with genuine appreciation. If people are willing to pay for the
work, talk about it, and show up for it, artists are far more likely to keep
creating and thrive.
[總結來說，我會說支持藝術家最好的方法，就是把實際支持和真誠欣賞結合起來。如
果人們願意為作品付費、願意主動談論它，也願意親身去支持它，藝術家就更有可能
持續創作，並且發展得更好。]

question.english: Do people read more nowadays?
question.chinese: 現代人是否比以前讀得更多？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do people read more nowadays?
現代人是否比以前讀得更多？

components.english: I’d say yes, people probably do read more nowadays if we include digital reading, although they may not read more books in the traditional sense.
components.chinese: 我會說，如果把數碼閱讀也算進去的話，現代人其實很可能比以前讀得更多，雖然他們未必在傳統意義上讀更多書。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I’d say yes, people probably do read more nowadays if we include digital
reading, although they may not read more books in the traditional sense.
[我會說，如果把數碼閱讀也算進去的話，現代人其實很可能比以前讀得更多，雖然他
們未必在傳統意義上讀更多書。]

components.english: The main reason is that people are surrounded by text almost all the time. They read messages, emails, news headlines, social media posts, captions, comments, and online articles from morning to night. In that sense, modern life is awash with words. Even when people are not sitting down with a novel, they are still reading in bits and pieces throughout the day. So, if we look at reading as a whole, it is hard to deny that people are constantly taking in written information.
