components.chinese: 主要原因是，現代人幾乎無時無刻都被文字包圍。他們從早到晚都在看訊息、電郵、新聞標題、社交媒體貼文、圖片說明、留言，以及網上文章。從這個角度來看，現代生活幾乎可以說是浸泡在文字之中。即使人們未必坐下來讀一本小說，他們仍然會在一天裡零零碎碎地不斷閱讀。所以，如果我們把閱讀整體來看，很難否認現代人其實一直都在吸收書面資訊。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that people are surrounded by text almost all
the time. They read messages, emails, news headlines, social media posts,
captions, comments, and online articles from morning to night. In that sense,
modern life is awash with words. Even when people are not sitting down with a
novel, they are still reading in bits and pieces throughout the day. So, if we look
at reading as a whole, it is hard to deny that people are constantly taking in
written information.
[主要原因是，現代人幾乎無時無刻都被文字包圍。他們從早到晚都在看訊息、電郵、
新聞標題、社交媒體貼文、圖片說明、留言，以及網上文章。從這個角度來看，現代生
活幾乎可以說是浸泡在文字之中。即使人們未必坐下來讀一本小說，他們仍然會在一
天裡零零碎碎地不斷閱讀。所以，如果我們把閱讀整體來看，很難否認現代人其實一
直都在吸收書面資訊。]

components.english: For example, a person may wake up and immediately read notifications on their phone, skim the news on the way to work, reply to emails during the day, and then scroll through social media at night. Piece by piece, that adds up to a huge amount of reading, even if it does not look like old-fashioned reading.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人早上起床後，可能立刻就會看手機通知；上班途中會瀏覽新聞；白天要回覆電郵；晚上又會滑社交媒體。這些零零碎碎的內容累積起來，其實已經構成了非常大量的閱讀，即使它看起來不像傳統的閱讀方式。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a person may wake up and immediately read
notifications on their phone, skim the news on the way to work, reply to emails
during the day, and then scroll through social media at night. Piece by piece,
that adds up to a huge amount of reading, even if it does not look like
old-fashioned reading.
[例如，一個人早上起床後，可能立刻就會看手機通知；上班途中會瀏覽新聞；白天要
回覆電郵；晚上又會滑社交媒體。這些零零碎碎的內容累積起來，其實已經構成了非
常大量的閱讀，即使它看起來不像傳統的閱讀方式。]

components.english: So, in overall terms, I do think people read more nowadays, but much of that reading happens on screens rather than on paper.
components.chinese: 所以，從整體來說，我的確認為現代人讀得更多了，只不過其中很大部分的閱讀都是在螢幕上進行，而不是在紙本上。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in overall terms, I do think people read more nowadays, but
much of that reading happens on screens rather than on paper.
[所以，從整體來說，我的確認為現代人讀得更多了，只不過其中很大部分的閱讀都是
在螢幕上進行，而不是在紙本上。]

components.english: That said, I do not think people necessarily read more deeply or more patiently than before, especially when it comes to books or long-form writing.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為現代人一定比以前讀得更深入、也更有耐性，尤其是在閱讀書籍或長篇文章方面。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think people necessarily read more deeply or more
patiently than before, especially when it comes to books or long-form writing.
[不過，我並不認為現代人一定比以前讀得更深入、也更有耐性，尤其是在閱讀書籍或
長篇文章方面。]

components.english: The problem is that a lot of modern reading is fast, fragmented, and driven by short attention spans. People tend to skim rather than savour. They may snack on information instead of really chewing it over. As a result, while the quantity of reading may have gone up, the depth of reading may well have gone down. To be honest, long-form reading now has to compete with videos, games, and endless online distractions, so it is no longer a walk in the park for many people to sit still with a serious book.
components.chinese: 問題在於，現代很多閱讀都是快速的、碎片化的，而且受到短暫專注力的影響。人們往往只是略讀，而不是細細品味。他們更像是在「吃零食」般地吸收資訊，而不是真正把內容咀嚼消化。結果就是，雖然閱讀的數量可能上升了，但閱讀的深度卻很可能下降了。老實說，現在的長篇閱讀還要和影片、遊戲，以及源源不絕的網上干擾競爭，所以對很多人來說，要靜下來好好讀一本嚴肅的書，已經不是一件輕鬆的事。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: The problem is that a lot of modern reading is fast,
fragmented, and driven by short attention spans. People tend to skim rather
than savour. They may snack on information instead of really chewing it over.
As a result, while the quantity of reading may have gone up, the depth of
reading may well have gone down. To be honest, long-form reading now has to
compete with videos, games, and endless online distractions, so it is no longer a
walk in the park for many people to sit still with a serious book.
[問題在於，現代很多閱讀都是快速的、碎片化的，而且受到短暫專注力的影響。人們
往往只是略讀，而不是細細品味。他們更像是在「吃零食」般地吸收資訊，而不是真正
把內容咀嚼消化。結果就是，雖然閱讀的數量可能上升了，但閱讀的深度卻很可能下
降了。老實說，現在的長篇閱讀還要和影片、遊戲，以及源源不絕的網上干擾競爭，所
以對很多人來說，要靜下來好好讀一本嚴肅的書，已經不是一件輕鬆的事。]

components.english: For instance, many people can spend hours reading posts and comments online, but still struggle to finish a long novel or a serious non-fiction book. That suggests people may be reading more in volume, while at the same time losing the habit of deep reading.
components.chinese: 例如，很多人可以花幾個小時去看網上的貼文和留言，但卻未必能順利讀完一本長篇小說，或者一本嚴肅的非小說書籍。這正正說明，人們可能在閱讀量上增加了，但同時也慢慢失去了深度閱讀的習慣。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, many people can spend hours reading posts and
comments online, but still struggle to finish a long novel or a serious non-fiction
book. That suggests people may be reading more in volume, while at the same
time losing the habit of deep reading.
[例如，很多人可以花幾個小時去看網上的貼文和留言，但卻未必能順利讀完一本長
篇小說，或者一本嚴肅的非小說書籍。這正正說明，人們可能在閱讀量上增加了，但同
時也慢慢失去了深度閱讀的習慣。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people do read more nowadays if we count all the text they consume every day. However, whether they read better, more deeply, or more meaningfully is a different question altogether.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，如果把人們每天接觸到的所有文字都算進去，現代人的確讀得更多。不過，他們是否讀得更好、更深入，或者更有意義，那又是另一回事了。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people do read more nowadays if we count all
the text they consume every day. However, whether they read better, more
deeply, or more meaningfully is a different question altogether.
[總結來說，我會說，如果把人們每天接觸到的所有文字都算進去，現代人的確讀得更
多。不過，他們是否讀得更好、更深入，或者更有意義，那又是另一回事了。]

question.english: Do you read before going to bed?
question.chinese: 你會在睡前閱讀嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you read before going to bed?
你會在睡前閱讀嗎？

components.english: Yes, I usually do, because reading before bed helps me slow down and switch off from the noise of the day.
components.chinese: 是的，我通常都會，因為睡前閱讀能幫助我慢下來，並且把自己從一天的忙亂之中抽離出來。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I usually do, because reading before bed helps me slow down and
switch off from the noise of the day.
[是的，我通常都會，因為睡前閱讀能幫助我慢下來，並且把自己從一天的忙亂之中抽
離出來。]

components.english: After a long day, my mind can still be buzzing with work, messages, or random thoughts, so reading gives me a chance to unwind. I find it much calmer than scrolling on my phone, because a good book settles the mind instead of overstimulating it. In a way, it acts like a soft landing at the end of the day.
components.chinese: 經過漫長的一天之後，我的腦袋往往仍然充滿工作、訊息，或者各種零碎想法，所以閱讀能讓我有機會放鬆下來。我覺得它比起滑手機要平靜得多，因為一本好書會讓心神安定，而不是讓人更加亢奮。某程度上，它就像一天結束前的一次溫柔降落。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: After a long day, my mind can still be buzzing with work,
messages, or random thoughts, so reading gives me a chance to unwind. I find it
much calmer than scrolling on my phone, because a good book settles the mind
instead of overstimulating it. In a way, it acts like a soft landing at the end of
the day.
[經過漫長的一天之後，我的腦袋往往仍然充滿工作、訊息，或者各種零碎想法，所以
閱讀能讓我有機會放鬆下來。我覺得它比起滑手機要平靜得多，因為一本好書會讓心
神安定，而不是讓人更加亢奮。某程度上，它就像一天結束前的一次溫柔降落。]

components.english: For example, I might read a few chapters of a novel or a short piece of non-fiction before sleeping. Even ten or fifteen minutes can help me clear my head and end the day on a quieter note.
components.chinese: 例如，我可能會在睡前讀幾章小說，或者看一小篇非小說類文章。即使只是十分鐘或十五分鐘，也能幫助我整理思緒，讓一天在比較平靜的氣氛中結束。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, I might read a few chapters of a novel or a short piece
of non-fiction before sleeping. Even ten or fifteen minutes can help me clear my
head and end the day on a quieter note.
[例如，我可能會在睡前讀幾章小說，或者看一小篇非小說類文章。即使只是十分鐘或
十五分鐘，也能幫助我整理思緒，讓一天在比較平靜的氣氛中結束。]

components.english: So yes, reading before bed is a habit I quite like, because it helps me relax and sleep in a better frame of mind.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我很喜歡睡前閱讀這個習慣，因為它能幫助我放鬆，也讓我帶著更好的心情入睡。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, reading before bed is a habit I quite like, because it helps
me relax and sleep in a better frame of mind.
[所以，是的，我很喜歡睡前閱讀這個習慣，因為它能幫助我放鬆，也讓我帶著更好的
心情入睡。]

components.english: That said, I do not read just anything before bed; I usually choose something light or enjoyable rather than something too heavy.
components.chinese: 不過，我睡前不會甚麼都讀；我通常會選一些比較輕鬆、比較容易投入的內容，而不是太沉重的東西。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not read just anything before bed; I usually choose
something light or enjoyable rather than something too heavy.
[不過，我睡前不會甚麼都讀；我通常會選一些比較輕鬆、比較容易投入的內容，而不
是太沉重的東西。]

components.english: If I read something too academic, too intense, or too emotionally draining, my brain may keep running instead of winding down. In that case, reading would defeat the purpose. So I tend to go for material that is gentle, interesting, and easy to sink into. It is a bit like easing my foot off the accelerator before the day comes to a full stop.
components.chinese: 如果我讀的內容太學術、太沉重，或者情緒上太消耗人，我的大腦反而可能停不下來，而不是慢慢平靜。在那種情況下，閱讀就反而失去了原本的作用。所以，我通常會選一些比較柔和、有趣，而且容易投入的內容。某程度上，這就像在一天完全停下來之前，先慢慢把腳從油門上放開。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: If I read something too academic, too intense, or too
emotionally draining, my brain may keep running instead of winding down. In
that case, reading would defeat the purpose. So I tend to go for material that is
gentle, interesting, and easy to sink into. It is a bit like easing my foot off the
accelerator before the day comes to a full stop.
[如果我讀的內容太學術、太沉重，或者情緒上太消耗人，我的大腦反而可能停不下來
，而不是慢慢平靜。在那種情況下，閱讀就反而失去了原本的作用。所以，我通常會選
一些比較柔和、有趣，而且容易投入的內容。某程度上，這就像在一天完全停下來之前
，先慢慢把腳從油門上放開。]

components.english: For instance, I would much rather read a short story, a travel piece, or a few pages of an easy-going book than a difficult report or upsetting news late at night. That way, I can enjoy the reading without lying awake afterwards.
components.chinese: 例如，到了晚上，我寧願看一篇短篇故事、一篇旅遊文章，或者一本輕鬆易讀的書的幾頁，也不太想去讀艱深的報告，或者令人心煩的新聞。那樣一來，我既能享受閱讀，也不會之後躺在床上還睡不著。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, I would much rather read a short story, a travel
piece, or a few pages of an easy-going book than a difficult report or upsetting
news late at night. That way, I can enjoy the reading without lying awake
afterwards.
[例如，到了晚上，我寧願看一篇短篇故事、一篇旅遊文章，或者一本輕鬆易讀的書的
幾頁，也不太想去讀艱深的報告，或者令人心煩的新聞。那樣一來，我既能享受閱讀，
也不會之後躺在床上還睡不著。]

components.english: Overall, yes, I do read before going to bed, but I treat it more as a way to unwind than as a time to tackle the heaviest material.
components.chinese: 總結來說，是的，我會在睡前閱讀，但我更把它當成一種放鬆方式，而不是拿來處理最沉重內容的時間。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, yes, I do read before going to bed, but I treat it more as
a way to unwind than as a time to tackle the heaviest material.
[總結來說，是的，我會在睡前閱讀，但我更把它當成一種放鬆方式，而不是拿來處理
最沉重內容的時間。]

question.english: In your opinion, how will e-books affect paper books?
question.chinese: 依你看，電子書將會怎樣影響紙本書？

source_lines.text: Topic: In your opinion, how will e-books affect paper books?
依你看，電子書將會怎樣影響紙本書？

components.english: In my view, e-books will definitely change the role of paper books, but I do not think they will replace them completely.
components.chinese: 在我看來，電子書一定會改變紙本書所扮演的角色，但我並不認為它們會把紙本書完全取代。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In my view, e-books will definitely change the role of paper books, but I
do not think they will replace them completely.
[在我看來，電子書一定會改變紙本書所扮演的角色，但我並不認為它們會把紙本書
完全取代。]

components.english: The biggest change is that e-books make reading more convenient, more portable, and often more affordable. People can carry hundreds of books on one device, download a title in seconds, and read almost anywhere without adding weight to their bags. In that sense, e-books have changed the game. They are especially attractive to people who travel a lot, move around frequently, or simply want instant access. So, as far as practicality is concerned, e-books are likely to take a larger slice of the pie.
components.chinese: 最大的改變在於，電子書令閱讀變得更方便、更容易攜帶，而且很多時候也更便宜。人們可以在一部裝置裡存放幾百本書，在幾秒鐘內下載一本新書，並且幾乎隨時隨地都可以閱讀，而不用讓書包變得沉甸甸。從這個角度來看，電子書的確改變了整個閱讀方式。對那些經常旅行、經常移動，或者只是想即時取得內容的人來說，它尤其有吸引力。所以，就實用性而言，電子書很可能會佔據越來越大的位置。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The biggest change is that e-books make reading more
convenient, more portable, and often more affordable. People can carry
hundreds of books on one device, download a title in seconds, and read almost
anywhere without adding weight to their bags. In that sense, e-books have
changed the game. They are especially attractive to people who travel a lot, move
around frequently, or simply want instant access. So, as far as practicality is
concerned, e-books are likely to take a larger slice of the pie.
[最大的改變在於，電子書令閱讀變得更方便、更容易攜帶，而且很多時候也更便宜。
人們可以在一部裝置裡存放幾百本書，在幾秒鐘內下載一本新書，並且幾乎隨時隨地
都可以閱讀，而不用讓書包變得沉甸甸。從這個角度來看，電子書的確改變了整個閱
讀方式。對那些經常旅行、經常移動，或者只是想即時取得內容的人來說，它尤其有吸
引力。所以，就實用性而言，電子書很可能會佔據越來越大的位置。]

components.english: For example, a university student may prefer e-books because they can highlight passages, search for keywords, and keep all their reading materials in one tablet. In the same way, someone who commutes every day may find it much easier to read on a phone or e-reader than to carry several thick books around.
components.chinese: 例如，一個大學生可能會偏好電子書，因為他們可以劃線、搜尋關鍵字，並把所有閱讀材料都放進同一部平板電腦裡。同樣地，一個每天都要通勤的人，也可能會覺得用手機或電子閱讀器看書，比帶著幾本厚厚的書四處走方便得多。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a university student may prefer e-books because they
can highlight passages, search for keywords, and keep all their reading materials
in one tablet. In the same way, someone who commutes every day may find it
much easier to read on a phone or e-reader than to carry several thick books
around.
[例如，一個大學生可能會偏好電子書，因為他們可以劃線、搜尋關鍵字，並把所有閱
讀材料都放進同一部平板電腦裡。同樣地，一個每天都要通勤的人，也可能會覺得用
手機或電子閱讀器看書，比帶著幾本厚厚的書四處走方便得多。]

components.english: So, one clear effect is that e-books will make reading more flexible and efficient, and that will inevitably reduce people’s reliance on paper books in some situations.
components.chinese: 所以，一個很明顯的影響就是，電子書會令閱讀變得更靈活、更有效率，而這無可避免會在某些情況下減少人們對紙本書的依賴。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear effect is that e-books will make reading more
flexible and efficient, and that will inevitably reduce people’s reliance on paper
books in some situations.
[所以，一個很明顯的影響就是，電子書會令閱讀變得更靈活、更有效率，而這無可避
免會在某些情況下減少人們對紙本書的依賴。]

components.english: That said, I believe paper books will still hold their ground, because they offer a reading experience that e-books cannot fully replicate.
components.chinese: 不過，我相信紙本書仍然會站得住腳，因為它們所提供的閱讀體驗，並不是電子書可以完全複製的。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I believe paper books will still hold their ground, because
they offer a reading experience that e-books cannot fully replicate.
[不過，我相信紙本書仍然會站得住腳，因為它們所提供的閱讀體驗，並不是電子書可
以完全複製的。]

components.english: For many people, paper books have a more tactile, focused, and even emotional quality. There is something special about turning real pages, seeing a book on a shelf, and reading without notifications or screen glare getting in the way. A paper book can feel like a companion, whereas an e-book is often more like a tool. On top of that, some readers still find it easier to concentrate and remember information when they read on paper. So, while e-books may steal some thunder, I do not think paper books will fade into thin air.
components.chinese: 對很多人來說，紙本書有一種更真實、更專注，甚至更帶感情的特質。翻動真正的書頁、看見一本書放在書架上，以及在沒有通知彈出或螢幕反光干擾的情況下閱讀，本身就有一種特別的感覺。紙本書有時像一個陪伴者，而電子書則更像一種工具。除此之外，有些讀者仍然覺得，在紙上閱讀時，自己更容易集中精神，也更容易記住內容。所以，雖然電子書可能會搶走部分風頭，我並不認為紙本書會就此消失得無影無蹤。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: For many people, paper books have a more tactile, focused, and
even emotional quality. There is something special about turning real pages,
seeing a book on a shelf, and reading without notifications or screen glare
getting in the way. A paper book can feel like a companion, whereas an e-book is
often more like a tool. On top of that, some readers still find it easier to
concentrate and remember information when they read on paper. So, while
e-books may steal some thunder, I do not think paper books will fade into thin
air.
[對很多人來說，紙本書有一種更真實、更專注，甚至更帶感情的特質。翻動真正的書
頁、看見一本書放在書架上，以及在沒有通知彈出或螢幕反光干擾的情況下閱讀，本
身就有一種特別的感覺。紙本書有時像一個陪伴者，而電子書則更像一種工具。除此
之外，有些讀者仍然覺得，在紙上閱讀時，自己更容易集中精神，也更容易記住內容。
所以，雖然電子書可能會搶走部分風頭，我並不認為紙本書會就此消失得無影無蹤。]

components.english: For instance, many people may happily read casual fiction on an e-reader, but still choose to buy printed versions of their favourite novels, children’s books, or beautifully designed editions. In those cases, the book is not just there to be read; it is also there to be kept, revisited, and cherished.
components.chinese: 例如，很多人可能很樂意用電子閱讀器看一般小說，但仍然會選擇購買自己最喜歡的小說、兒童讀物，或者設計精美的紙本版本。在這些情況下，一本書的存在不只是為了被閱讀；它也是用來保存、重溫，以及珍惜的。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, many people may happily read casual fiction on an
e-reader, but still choose to buy printed versions of their favourite novels,
children’s books, or beautifully designed editions. In those cases, the book is not
just there to be read; it is also there to be kept, revisited, and cherished.
[例如，很多人可能很樂意用電子閱讀器看一般小說，但仍然會選擇購買自己最喜歡
的小說、兒童讀物，或者設計精美的紙本版本。在這些情況下，一本書的存在不只是
為了被閱讀；它也是用來保存、重溫，以及珍惜的。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say e-books will push paper books to adapt rather than disappear. They will probably take over some practical functions, but paper books are still likely to survive because they offer a richer and more personal reading experience.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說電子書會迫使紙本書改變自己的角色，而不是令它們消失。電子書大概會接手一些較實用的功能，但紙本書仍然很可能會繼續存在，因為它們提供的是一種更豐富、也更個人的閱讀體驗。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say e-books will push paper books to adapt rather
than disappear. They will probably take over some practical functions, but
paper books are still likely to survive because they offer a richer and more
personal reading experience.
[總結來說，我會說電子書會迫使紙本書改變自己的角色，而不是令它們消失。電子書
大概會接手一些較實用的功能，但紙本書仍然很可能會繼續存在，因為它們提供的是
一種更豐富、也更個人的閱讀體驗。]

question.english: What’s the difference between films and books?
question.chinese: 電影和書本有甚麼分別？

source_lines.text: Topic: What’s the difference between films and books?
電影和書本有甚麼分別？

components.english: I think the biggest difference is that books usually give more depth and more room for imagination, whereas films tend to be more immediate and visually powerful.
components.chinese: 我認為，最大的分別在於，書本通常能提供更深的內容和更大的想像空間，而電影則往往更直接，也更具視覺衝擊力。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think the biggest difference is that books usually give more depth and
more room for imagination, whereas films tend to be more immediate and
visually powerful.
[我認為，最大的分別在於，書本通常能提供更深的內容和更大的想像空間，而電影則
往往更直接，也更具視覺衝擊力。]

components.english: When people read a book, they have to picture the characters, settings, and emotions in their own minds, so reading is often a slower and more personal experience. A book can also go deeper into someone’s thoughts, background, and inner conflict. Films, by contrast, put everything right in front of you through images, sound, music, and acting, so they can hit you much more quickly. In a way, books ask the imagination to do more of the heavy lifting, while films bring the world to life for you straight away.
components.chinese: 當人們讀一本書時，他們需要在自己腦海裡想像角色、場景和情感，所以閱讀往往是一種比較慢、也比較個人的體驗。書本也通常能更深入地描寫人物的思想、背景，以及內心矛盾。相比之下，電影會透過畫面、聲音、音樂和演技，把一切直接呈現在你眼前，所以它往往能更快地打動人。某程度上，書本需要讀者用想像力做更多功夫，而電影則會立刻把整個世界活生生地帶到你面前。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: When people read a book, they have to picture the characters,
settings, and emotions in their own minds, so reading is often a slower and
more personal experience. A book can also go deeper into someone’s thoughts,
background, and inner conflict. Films, by contrast, put everything right in front
of you through images, sound, music, and acting, so they can hit you much more
quickly. In a way, books ask the imagination to do more of the heavy lifting,
while films bring the world to life for you straight away.
[當人們讀一本書時，他們需要在自己腦海裡想像角色、場景和情感，所以閱讀往往是
一種比較慢、也比較個人的體驗。書本也通常能更深入地描寫人物的思想、背景，以及
內心矛盾。相比之下，電影會透過畫面、聲音、音樂和演技，把一切直接呈現在你眼前
，所以它往往能更快地打動人。某程度上，書本需要讀者用想像力做更多功夫，而電影
則會立刻把整個世界活生生地帶到你面前。]

components.english: For example, if the same story appears both as a novel and as a film, the novel may spend pages exploring what a character is thinking, while the film has to show that emotion through facial expression, dialogue, or music. As a result, the book may feel richer in detail, but the film may feel more vivid in the moment.
components.chinese: 例如，如果同一個故事同時有小說和電影版本，小說可能會花很多篇幅去描寫角色在想甚麼，而電影則必須透過表情、對白或音樂來呈現那種情感。結果就是，書本可能會顯得更細膩、更豐富，但電影在當下的感受往往會更鮮明。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if the same story appears both as a novel and as a film,
the novel may spend pages exploring what a character is thinking, while the film
has to show that emotion through facial expression, dialogue, or music. As a
result, the book may feel richer in detail, but the film may feel more vivid in the
moment.
[例如，如果同一個故事同時有小說和電影版本，小說可能會花很多篇幅去描寫角色
在想甚麼，而電影則必須透過表情、對白或音樂來呈現那種情感。結果就是，書本可能
會顯得更細膩、更豐富，但電影在當下的感受往往會更鮮明。]

components.english: So, one major difference is that books usually offer more depth and freedom of imagination, while films deliver the story in a more direct and sensory way.
components.chinese: 所以，其中一個主要分別就是，書本通常提供更多深度和想像自由，而電影則以更直接、更多感官的方式去傳遞故事。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major difference is that books usually offer more depth
and freedom of imagination, while films deliver the story in a more direct and
sensory way.
[所以，其中一個主要分別就是，書本通常提供更多深度和想像自由，而電影則以更直
接、更多感官的方式去傳遞故事。]

components.english: Another difference is that films are generally more accessible and faster to consume, whereas books require more time, patience, and concentration.
components.chinese: 另一個分別是，電影通常更容易接觸，也更快就能看完；相反，書本則需要更多時間、耐性和專注力。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another difference is that films are generally more accessible and
faster to consume, whereas books require more time, patience, and
concentration.
[另一個分別是，電影通常更容易接觸，也更快就能看完；相反，書本則需要更多時間、
耐性和專注力。]

components.english: Most people can sit down and watch a film in two hours, but finishing a book may take days or even weeks. That is why films often appeal to a wider audience. They fit more easily into busy modern life. Books, however, usually ask more of the reader, and that can be both their strength and their weakness. They may not grab people instantly, but they often leave a deeper footprint. So, to me, films are easier to step into, while books are easier to sink into.
components.chinese: 大多數人都可以花兩個小時坐下來看完一部電影，但讀完一本書卻可能要花上幾天，甚至幾個星期。這也正是為甚麼電影通常能吸引更廣泛的觀眾，因為它更容易配合現代忙碌的生活。不過，書本通常對讀者要求更高，而這同時也是它的優點和缺點。它未必能立刻抓住所有人，但往往能留下更深的痕跡。所以，在我看來，電影比較容易讓人走進去，而書本則比較容易讓人沉下去。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Most people can sit down and watch a film in two hours, but
finishing a book may take days or even weeks. That is why films often appeal to
a wider audience. They fit more easily into busy modern life. Books, however,
usually ask more of the reader, and that can be both their strength and their
weakness. They may not grab people instantly, but they often leave a deeper
footprint. So, to me, films are easier to step into, while books are easier to sink
into.
[大多數人都可以花兩個小時坐下來看完一部電影，但讀完一本書卻可能要花上幾天
，甚至幾個星期。這也正是為甚麼電影通常能吸引更廣泛的觀眾，因為它更容易配合
現代忙碌的生活。不過，書本通常對讀者要求更高，而這同時也是它的優點和缺點。它
未必能立刻抓住所有人，但往往能留下更深的痕跡。所以，在我看來，電影比較容易讓
人走進去，而書本則比較容易讓人沉下去。]

components.english: For instance, someone who is tired after work may prefer to watch a film because it feels effortless, whereas reading a serious novel may require more mental energy. Even so, that same reader may remember the book for much longer because they spent more time living inside it.
components.chinese: 例如，一個下班後已經很疲倦的人，可能會比較想看電影，因為那種感覺較輕鬆；相反，閱讀一本嚴肅的小說則可能需要更多精神。即便如此，那位讀者之後卻可能會更長久地記得那本書，因為他花了更多時間活在那個世界裡。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, someone who is tired after work may prefer to watch a
film because it feels effortless, whereas reading a serious novel may require more
mental energy. Even so, that same reader may remember the book for much
longer because they spent more time living inside it.
[例如，一個下班後已經很疲倦的人，可能會比較想看電影，因為那種感覺較輕鬆；相
反，閱讀一本嚴肅的小說則可能需要更多精神。即便如此，那位讀者之後卻可能會更
長久地記得那本書，因為他花了更多時間活在那個世界裡。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say films and books tell stories in very different ways. Films are quicker, more visual, and more immediate, while books are deeper, more reflective, and more dependent on the reader’s imagination.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說電影和書本是用很不同的方式去說故事的。電影更快、更視覺化，也更直接；而書本則更深入、更值得反思，也更依賴讀者本身的想像力。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say films and books tell stories in very different
ways. Films are quicker, more visual, and more immediate, while books are
deeper, more reflective, and more dependent on the reader’s imagination.
[總結來說，我會說電影和書本是用很不同的方式去說故事的。電影更快、更視覺化，
也更直接；而書本則更深入、更值得反思，也更依賴讀者本身的想像力。]

question.english: Do you like reading the traditional literature of your country?
question.chinese: 你喜歡閱讀你國家的傳統文學嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you like reading the traditional literature of your country?
你喜歡閱讀你國家的傳統文學嗎？

components.english: Yes, I do, although I would say I appreciate it more than I read it casually.
components.chinese: 是的，我的確喜歡，不過更準確地說，我對它是欣賞多於隨意閱讀。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I do, although I would say I appreciate it more than I read it
casually.
[是的，我的確喜歡，不過更準確地說，我對它是欣賞多於隨意閱讀。]

components.english: The main reason is that traditional literature gives me a window into the cultural roots of Hong Kong and the wider Cantonese-speaking world. It carries history, values, rhythm, and ways of thinking that modern writing does not always preserve. In that sense, it feels a bit like listening to old voices that still have something meaningful to say. I find that fascinating, because it reminds me that culture does not appear out of thin air; it is built layer by layer over time.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，傳統文學讓我能夠看見香港，以及更廣泛粵語文化圈的文化根源。它承載著歷史、價值觀、節奏感，以及一些現代文字未必完整保存下來的思維方式。從這個角度來看，它有點像在聆聽一些古老的聲音，而那些聲音直到今天仍然有值得人細味的地方。我會覺得這很吸引，因為它提醒我，文化並不是憑空出現的，而是一層一層慢慢累積起來的。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that traditional literature gives me a window
into the cultural roots of Hong Kong and the wider Cantonese-speaking world.
It carries history, values, rhythm, and ways of thinking that modern writing
does not always preserve. In that sense, it feels a bit like listening to old voices
that still have something meaningful to say. I find that fascinating, because it
reminds me that culture does not appear out of thin air; it is built layer by layer
over time.
[主要原因是，傳統文學讓我能夠看見香港，以及更廣泛粵語文化圈的文化根源。它承
載著歷史、價值觀、節奏感，以及一些現代文字未必完整保存下來的思維方式。從這個
角度來看，它有點像在聆聽一些古老的聲音，而那些聲音直到今天仍然有值得人細味
的地方。我會覺得這很吸引，因為它提醒我，文化並不是憑空出現的，而是一層一層慢
慢累積起來的。]

components.english: For example, when I come across traditional Cantonese opera lyrics or older literary passages connected with works like The Flower Princess, I can really feel the elegance of the language and the emotional weight behind it. Even if I do not read that kind of writing every day, it still leaves a lasting impression on me.
components.chinese: 例如，當我接觸到粵劇唱詞，或者像《帝女花》這類作品相關的較傳統文字時，我真的能感受到那種語言的雅致，以及背後濃厚的情感分量。即使我不是每天都讀這一類作品，它仍然會在我心裡留下很深的印象。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, when I come across traditional Cantonese opera lyrics
or older literary passages connected with works like The Flower Princess, I can
really feel the elegance of the language and the emotional weight behind it. Even
if I do not read that kind of writing every day, it still leaves a lasting impression
on me.
[例如，當我接觸到粵劇唱詞，或者像《帝女花》這類作品相關的較傳統文字時，我真的
能感受到那種語言的雅致，以及背後濃厚的情感分量。即使我不是每天都讀這一類作
品，它仍然會在我心裡留下很深的印象。]

components.english: So yes, I do like reading traditional literature, mainly because it helps me connect with the cultural heartbeat of my own place.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我喜歡閱讀傳統文學，主要因為它能幫助我和自己所屬地方的文化脈搏建立連結。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I do like reading traditional literature, mainly because it
helps me connect with the cultural heartbeat of my own place.
[所以，是的，我喜歡閱讀傳統文學，主要因為它能幫助我和自己所屬地方的文化脈搏
建立連結。]

components.english: That said, I would not pretend that traditional literature is always easy or relaxing to read.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不會假裝傳統文學總是容易讀、或者讀起來總是很輕鬆。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I would not pretend that traditional literature is always
easy or relaxing to read.
[不過，我也不會假裝傳統文學總是容易讀、或者讀起來總是很輕鬆。]

components.english: To be honest, the language can sometimes be dense, highly stylised, and quite far removed from the way people speak today. That means reading it can feel like hard work, especially if the text is full of historical references or classical expressions. In other words, it is not always the kind of thing you curl up with at the end of the day. Still, I think that difficulty is part of its charm. It slows you down, makes you dig a little deeper, and reminds you that not everything valuable comes wrapped in convenience.
components.chinese: 老實說，那種語言有時會相當濃縮、很有程式感，而且和現代人說話的方式距離頗遠。這就意味著，閱讀它有時確實需要花一點力氣，尤其當文本裡充滿歷史典故或古典表達時更是如此。換句話說，它未必總是那種可以讓人晚上輕輕鬆鬆捧著來讀的東西。不過，我反而覺得這種難度正正是它魅力的一部分。它會令你慢下來、逼你多想一步，也提醒你：有價值的東西，未必總是包裝得方便易入口。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, the language can sometimes be dense, highly
stylised, and quite far removed from the way people speak today. That means
reading it can feel like hard work, especially if the text is full of historical
references or classical expressions. In other words, it is not always the kind of
thing you curl up with at the end of the day. Still, I think that difficulty is part
of its charm. It slows you down, makes you dig a little deeper, and reminds you
that not everything valuable comes wrapped in convenience.
[老實說，那種語言有時會相當濃縮、很有程式感，而且和現代人說話的方式距離頗
遠。這就意味著，閱讀它有時確實需要花一點力氣，尤其當文本裡充滿歷史典故或古
典表達時更是如此。換句話說，它未必總是那種可以讓人晚上輕輕鬆鬆捧著來讀的東
西。不過，我反而覺得這種難度正正是它魅力的一部分。它會令你慢下來、逼你多想一
步，也提醒你：有價值的東西，未必總是包裝得方便易入口。]

components.english: For instance, if I am tired, I would probably choose a modern novel or a short article rather than a dense traditional text. However, when I am in the right mood, reading older literature feels rewarding, almost like uncovering a layer of cultural memory that would otherwise stay buried.
components.chinese: 例如，如果我很累，我大概會選一本現代小說，或者一篇短文章，而不會選擇一篇艱深的傳統文本。不過，當我處於合適的心情時，閱讀較古老的文學其實會帶來很大的滿足感，幾乎像是在挖掘一層原本可能被埋沒的文化記憶。

or a short article rather than a dense traditional text. However, when I am in
the right mood, reading older literature feels rewarding, almost like uncovering
a layer of cultural memory that would otherwise stay buried.

滿足感，幾乎像是在挖掘一層原本可能被埋沒的文化記憶。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say I do enjoy the traditional literature of Hong Kong, but in a thoughtful way rather than in a purely casual one. It may not always be light reading, but it is certainly rich reading.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說我確實喜歡香港的傳統文學，只不過那是一種帶著思考去欣賞的喜歡，而不是純粹輕鬆隨手地讀。它未必總是輕鬆的閱讀，但絕對是很有厚度的閱讀。

喜歡，而不是純粹輕鬆隨手地讀。它未必總是輕鬆的閱讀，但絕對是很有厚度的閱

question.english: Do you prefer books or movies?
question.chinese: 你比較喜歡書本還是電影？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you prefer books or movies?
主題：你比較喜歡書本還是電影？

components.english: Personally, I prefer books, because they usually give me more depth, more imagination, and more time to live inside a story.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我比較喜歡書本，因為它通常能給我更深的內容、更大的想像空間，以及更多時間去活在一個故事裡。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I prefer books, because they usually give me more depth,
more imagination, and more time to live inside a story.
[就我個人而言，我比較喜歡書本，因為它通常能給我更深的內容、更大的想像空間，
以及更多時間去活在一個故事裡。]

components.english: When I read, I can move at my own pace and picture the characters, settings, and emotions in my own way. A book does not just hand everything to you on a plate; it asks you to meet it halfway. That is what I enjoy. In a sense, reading feels less like watching a story unfold and more like stepping into it. A good novel can get under your skin and stay there for quite a while.
components.chinese: 當我閱讀時，我可以按照自己的節奏前進，也可以用自己的方式去想像人物、場景和情感。一本書不會把所有東西直接端到你面前；它會要求你主動走近它一半，而我正正很享受這一點。某程度上，閱讀不像是在旁邊看著一個故事展開，而更像是真正走進那個故事裡。一部好的小說往往能深入人心，而且會在心裡停留很久。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: When I read, I can move at my own pace and picture the
characters, settings, and emotions in my own way. A book does not just hand
everything to you on a plate; it asks you to meet it halfway. That is what I enjoy.
In a sense, reading feels less like watching a story unfold and more like stepping
into it. A good novel can get under your skin and stay there for quite a while.
[當我閱讀時，我可以按照自己的節奏前進，也可以用自己的方式去想像人物、場景和
情感。一本書不會把所有東西直接端到你面前；它會要求你主動走近它一半，而我正
正很享受這一點。某程度上，閱讀不像是在旁邊看著一個故事展開，而更像是真正走
進那個故事裡。一部好的小說往往能深入人心，而且會在心裡停留很久。]

components.english: For example, when I read a strong novel, I often find myself thinking about the characters long after I have put the book down. Sometimes I even imagine scenes differently from the way another reader would, and that makes the experience feel more personal and more alive.
components.chinese: 例如，當我讀到一本很出色的小說時，我往往會在合上書之後，仍然一直想著裡面的人物。有時候，我甚至會用和其他讀者完全不同的方式去想像某些場景，而這正正令整個閱讀經驗顯得更個人化，也更有生命力。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, when I read a strong novel, I often find myself
thinking about the characters long after I have put the book down. Sometimes I
even imagine scenes differently from the way another reader would, and that
makes the experience feel more personal and more alive.
[例如，當我讀到一本很出色的小說時，我往往會在合上書之後，仍然一直想著裡面的
人物。有時候，我甚至會用和其他讀者完全不同的方式去想像某些場景，而這正正令
整個閱讀經驗顯得更個人化，也更有生命力。]

components.english: So, if I had to choose one, I would go for books, mainly because they feel more immersive and more lasting.
components.chinese: 所以，如果一定要我選一樣，我會選書本，主要因為它給我的感覺更有沉浸感，也更能留下長久的印象。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if I had to choose one, I would go for books, mainly because
they feel more immersive and more lasting.
[所以，如果一定要我選一樣，我會選書本，主要因為它給我的感覺更有沉浸感，也更
能留下長久的印象。]

components.english: That said, I can completely see why many people prefer movies, because films have strengths that books simply cannot match.
components.chinese: 不過，我也完全明白為甚麼很多人會比較喜歡電影，因為電影確實有一些書本無法取代的優勢。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I can completely see why many people prefer movies,
because films have strengths that books simply cannot match.
[不過，我也完全明白為甚麼很多人會比較喜歡電影，因為電影確實有一些書本無法
取代的優勢。]

components.english: A film can bring a story to life through acting, music, visuals, and pacing, so it often hits harder and faster. Sometimes a powerful scene, a piece of music, or even a look on an actor’s face can say more than a whole page of description. On top of that, movies are easier to fit into a busy life. After a long day, it is often easier to sink into a film than to wrestle with a demanding book. So, while books win my heart, films can still steal the show in the right moment.
components.chinese: 電影可以透過演技、音樂、畫面和節奏，把一個故事真正活生生地帶到人面前，所以它往往能更快、更強烈地打動人。有時候，一個震撼的場面、一段音樂，甚至演員臉上的一個眼神，都可能比整整一頁描寫更有力量。除此之外，電影也更容易配合忙碌的生活。在漫長的一天之後，人通常比較容易投入一部電影，而不是和一本需要高度專注的書苦戰。所以，雖然書本更能打動我的心，但電影在適當的時候仍然很有可能成為全場焦點。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: A film can bring a story to life through acting, music, visuals,
and pacing, so it often hits harder and faster. Sometimes a powerful scene, a
piece of music, or even a look on an actor’s face can say more than a whole page
of description. On top of that, movies are easier to fit into a busy life. After a
long day, it is often easier to sink into a film than to wrestle with a demanding
book. So, while books win my heart, films can still steal the show in the right
moment.
[電影可以透過演技、音樂、畫面和節奏，把一個故事真正活生生地帶到人面前，所以
它往往能更快、更強烈地打動人。有時候，一個震撼的場面、一段音樂，甚至演員臉上
的一個眼神，都可能比整整一頁描寫更有力量。除此之外，電影也更容易配合忙碌的
生活。在漫長的一天之後，人通常比較容易投入一部電影，而不是和一本需要高度專
注的書苦戰。所以，雖然書本更能打動我的心，但電影在適當的時候仍然很有可能成
為全場焦點。]

components.english: For instance, if I am tired after work, I would probably choose a well-made film over a dense novel, simply because it feels more effortless. But if I really want a story I can savour slowly and carry with me, then I would still reach for a book.
components.chinese: 例如，如果我下班後已經很累，我大概會選一部拍得很好的電影，而不是一本艱深的小說，因為那種感覺會比較不費力。不過，如果我真正想要的是一個可以慢慢品味、而且能長時間留在心裡的故事，那我仍然會伸手去拿一本書。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if I am tired after work, I would probably choose a
well-made film over a dense novel, simply because it feels more effortless. But if I
really want a story I can savour slowly and carry with me, then I would still
reach for a book.
[例如，如果我下班後已經很累，我大概會選一部拍得很好的電影，而不是一本艱深的
小說，因為那種感覺會比較不費力。不過，如果我真正想要的是一個可以慢慢品味、而
且能長時間留在心裡的故事，那我仍然會伸手去拿一本書。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say I prefer books, but not by a mile. Books feed the imagination, whereas movies deliver instant impact. It really depends on my mood, but if push came to shove, I would still pick books.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說我比較喜歡書本，但也不是一面倒地偏愛。書本滋養想像力，而電影則帶來即時的衝擊力。最終還是要看當下的心情，不過如果真的要我二選一，我仍然會選書本。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say I prefer books, but not by a mile. Books feed the
imagination, whereas movies deliver instant impact. It really depends on my
mood, but if push came to shove, I would still pick books.
[總結來說，我會說我比較喜歡書本，但也不是一面倒地偏愛。書本滋養想像力，而電
影則帶來即時的衝擊力。最終還是要看當下的心情，不過如果真的要我二選一，我仍
然會選書本。]

question.english: Do you think it is important to read the book before watching the movie version of it?
question.chinese: 你認為在看電影改編版本之前，先讀原著是否重要？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think it is important to read the book before watching the movie
version of it?
你認為在看電影改編版本之前，先讀原著是否重要？

components.english: Personally, I think it is helpful to read the book first, but I would not say it is absolutely necessary.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我認為先讀原著是有幫助的，但我不會說那是絕對必要的。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I think it is helpful to read the book first, but I would not
say it is absolutely necessary.
[就我個人而言，我認為先讀原著是有幫助的，但我不會說那是絕對必要的。]

components.english: The main reason is that books usually give more depth to the story. They allow readers to understand the characters’ thoughts, background, and emotional struggles in a much fuller way, whereas films often have to trim the fat and move at a faster pace. In that sense, reading the book first can lay the groundwork and make the film feel richer afterwards. You are not just watching events unfold; you are seeing a world you already know come to life.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，書本通常能為故事提供更深的內容。它能讓讀者更完整地理解角色的思想、背景，以及情感上的掙扎；相比之下，電影往往必須刪減內容，並以更快的節奏推進。從這個角度來看，先讀原著可以先把基礎打好，之後看電影時也會覺得內容更豐富。那時候你不只是看著情節發生，而是在看一個你早已熟悉的世界活生生地展現出來。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that books usually give more depth to the
story. They allow readers to understand the characters’ thoughts, background,
and emotional struggles in a much fuller way, whereas films often have to trim
the fat and move at a faster pace. In that sense, reading the book first can lay the
groundwork and make the film feel richer afterwards. You are not just watching
events unfold; you are seeing a world you already know come to life.
[主要原因是，書本通常能為故事提供更深的內容。它能讓讀者更完整地理解角色的
思想、背景，以及情感上的掙扎；相比之下，電影往往必須刪減內容，並以更快的節奏
推進。從這個角度來看，先讀原著可以先把基礎打好，之後看電影時也會覺得內容更
豐富。那時候你不只是看著情節發生，而是在看一個你早已熟悉的世界活生生地展現
出來。]

components.english: For example, when someone reads a novel first, they may already understand why a character makes certain choices, even if the film only hints at it. As a result, the movie can carry more emotional weight, because the viewer is reading between the lines instead of just following the plot on the surface.
components.chinese: 例如，當一個人先讀過小說之後，即使電影只是輕輕帶過某些情節，他也可能早已明白角色為甚麼會作出某些選擇。結果就是，那部電影會顯得更有情感分量，因為觀眾不只是在表面上跟著劇情走，而是能看懂字裡行間的意思。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, when someone reads a novel first, they may already
understand why a character makes certain choices, even if the film only hints at
it. As a result, the movie can carry more emotional weight, because the viewer is
reading between the lines instead of just following the plot on the surface.
[例如，當一個人先讀過小說之後，即使電影只是輕輕帶過某些情節，他也可能早已明
白角色為甚麼會作出某些選擇。結果就是，那部電影會顯得更有情感分量，因為觀眾
不只是在表面上跟著劇情走，而是能看懂字裡行間的意思。]

components.english: So yes, I do think reading the book first can be important, mainly because it deepens the experience and gives the story more texture.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我的確認為先讀原著可以是重要的，主要因為它能加深整體體驗，也讓故事變得更有層次。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I do think reading the book first can be important,
mainly because it deepens the experience and gives the story more texture.
[所以，是的，我的確認為先讀原著可以是重要的，主要因為它能加深整體體驗，也讓
故事變得更有層次。]

components.english: That said, I do not think people must read the book first, because a film should also be able to stand on its own two feet.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為人們一定非要先讀書不可，因為一部電影本身也應該能夠獨立成立。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think people must read the book first, because a
film should also be able to stand on its own two feet.
[不過，我並不認為人們一定非要先讀書不可，因為一部電影本身也應該能夠獨立成

components.english: To be honest, sometimes reading the book first can be a double-edged sword. It may enrich the experience, but it can also raise expectations sky-high and make people focus too much on what has been cut or changed. In some cases, that takes the wind out of the film before it even has a fair chance. If viewers go in with an open mind, they may enjoy the adaptation for what it is rather than constantly comparing it page by page.
components.chinese: 老實說，先讀原著有時也是一把雙刃劍。它固然可能令觀影體驗更豐富，但同時也可能把期待拉得太高，令觀眾過度執著於哪些內容被刪掉了、哪些地方被改動了。在某些情況下，這甚至會在電影真正展開之前，就先把它的吸引力削弱了。如果觀眾能抱著較開放的心態去看，他們反而可能更能欣賞改編作品本身，而不是一頁一頁地拿它和原著比較。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, sometimes reading the book first can be a
double-edged sword. It may enrich the experience, but it can also raise
expectations sky-high and make people focus too much on what has been cut or
changed. In some cases, that takes the wind out of the film before it even has a
fair chance. If viewers go in with an open mind, they may enjoy the adaptation
for what it is rather than constantly comparing it page by page.
[老實說，先讀原著有時也是一把雙刃劍。它固然可能令觀影體驗更豐富，但同時也可
能把期待拉得太高，令觀眾過度執著於哪些內容被刪掉了、哪些地方被改動了。在某
些情況下，這甚至會在電影真正展開之前，就先把它的吸引力削弱了。如果觀眾能抱
著較開放的心態去看，他們反而可能更能欣賞改編作品本身，而不是一頁一頁地拿它
和原著比較。]

components.english: For instance, some people watch a film adaptation first, enjoy the story, and then read the book afterwards to explore the deeper layers. That can work just as well, because the movie sparks interest, and the book then fills in the gaps.
components.chinese: 例如，有些人會先看電影改編版本，先喜歡上那個故事，之後再去讀原著，進一步探索更深的層次。這樣其實也一樣可行，因為電影先點燃了興趣，而書本則在之後把那些空白補上。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, some people watch a film adaptation first, enjoy the
story, and then read the book afterwards to explore the deeper layers. That can
work just as well, because the movie sparks interest, and the book then fills in
the gaps.
[例如，有些人會先看電影改編版本，先喜歡上那個故事，之後再去讀原著，進一步探
索更深的層次。這樣其實也一樣可行，因為電影先點燃了興趣，而書本則在之後把那
些空白補上。]

components.english: Overall, I would say it is beneficial to read the book before watching the movie, but it is not essential. The best approach really depends on what kind of experience you want: deeper understanding first, or a fresher sense of surprise.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說在看電影之前先讀原著是有益的，但並不是必要條件。最好的做法其實取決於你想要的是哪一種體驗：是先有更深的理解，還是先保留一份更新鮮的驚喜感。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say it is beneficial to read the book before
watching the movie, but it is not essential. The best approach really depends on
what kind of experience you want: deeper understanding first, or a fresher sense
of surprise.
[總結來說，我會說在看電影之前先讀原著是有益的，但並不是必要條件。最好的做法
其實取決於你想要的是哪一種體驗：是先有更深的理解，還是先保留一份更新鮮的驚
喜感。]

question.english: Do boys and girls like the same kinds of books?
question.chinese: 男孩和女孩喜歡同一類型的書嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do boys and girls like the same kinds of books?
男孩和女孩喜歡同一類型的書嗎？

components.english: I would say partly yes, because boys and girls often enjoy many of the same kinds of books, especially when the story is exciting, funny, or emotionally engaging.
components.chinese: 我會說某程度上是的，因為當一本書的故事夠精彩、夠有趣，或者在情感上很能吸引人時，男孩和女孩其實常常都會喜歡相似類型的書。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I would say partly yes, because boys and girls often enjoy many of the
same kinds of books, especially when the story is exciting, funny, or emotionally
engaging.
[我會說某程度上是的，因為當一本書的故事夠精彩、夠有趣，或者在情感上很能吸引
人時，男孩和女孩其實常常都會喜歡相似類型的書。]

components.english: In my view, good stories cut across gender more than people sometimes assume. Adventure, fantasy, mystery, and humour tend to appeal to a wide range of readers, because the real hook is often the plot, the characters, and the emotional pull of the story. If a book is well written and really draws people in, boys and girls are both likely to turn the pages. So, to me, it would be painting with too broad a brush to say their tastes are completely different.
components.chinese: 在我看來，好故事往往比人們想像中更能跨越性別界線。冒險、奇幻、推理，以及幽默類型的作品，通常都能吸引很廣泛的讀者，因為真正抓住人的，往往是情節、人物，以及故事本身帶來的情感吸引力。如果一本書寫得好，而且真的能把人拉進去，那麼男孩和女孩都很可能會一直讀下去。所以，在我看來，如果直接說他們的口味完全不同，其實是過度一概而論了。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: In my view, good stories cut across gender more than people
sometimes assume. Adventure, fantasy, mystery, and humour tend to appeal to a
wide range of readers, because the real hook is often the plot, the characters, and
the emotional pull of the story. If a book is well written and really draws people
in, boys and girls are both likely to turn the pages. So, to me, it would be
painting with too broad a brush to say their tastes are completely different.
[在我看來，好故事往往比人們想像中更能跨越性別界線。冒險、奇幻、推理，以及幽
默類型的作品，通常都能吸引很廣泛的讀者，因為真正抓住人的，往往是情節、人物，
以及故事本身帶來的情感吸引力。如果一本書寫得好，而且真的能把人拉進去，那麼
男孩和女孩都很可能會一直讀下去。所以，在我看來，如果直接說他們的口味完全不
同，其實是過度一概而論了。]

components.english: For example, when a popular fantasy series or mystery novel comes out, it is common to see both boys and girls reading it, talking about it, and recommending it to their friends. In cases like that, the story itself does the heavy lifting, not the reader’s gender.
components.chinese: 例如，當一本受歡迎的奇幻系列或推理小說推出時，我們很常看到男孩和女孩都在閱讀、討論，甚至推薦給朋友。在這種情況下，真正起作用的是故事本身，而不是讀者的性別。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, when a popular fantasy series or mystery novel comes
out, it is common to see both boys and girls reading it, talking about it, and
recommending it to their friends. In cases like that, the story itself does the
heavy lifting, not the reader’s gender.
[例如，當一本受歡迎的奇幻系列或推理小說推出時，我們很常看到男孩和女孩都在
閱讀、討論，甚至推薦給朋友。在這種情況下，真正起作用的是故事本身，而不是讀者
的性別。]

components.english: So yes, I do think boys and girls can enjoy many of the same books, especially when the writing is strong and the story has broad appeal.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我的確認為男孩和女孩可以喜歡很多相同的書，特別是當作品寫得出色，而且故事本身有廣泛吸引力的時候。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I do think boys and girls can enjoy many of the same
books, especially when the writing is strong and the story has broad appeal.
[所以，是的，我的確認為男孩和女孩可以喜歡很多相同的書，特別是當作品寫得出色
，而且故事本身有廣泛吸引力的時候。]

components.english: That said, I also think there can be some differences in reading preferences, but those differences are shaped more by personality and social influence than by gender alone.
components.chinese: 不過，我也認為閱讀口味之間確實可能存在一些差異，只是那些差異與其說是單純由性別決定，倒不如說更多是受到個性和社會影響所塑造。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I also think there can be some differences in reading
preferences, but those differences are shaped more by personality and social
influence than by gender alone.
[不過，我也認為閱讀口味之間確實可能存在一些差異，只是那些差異與其說是單純
由性別決定，倒不如說更多是受到個性和社會影響所塑造。]

components.english: Some boys may lean towards action, science fiction, or sports-related books, while some girls may be more drawn to relationship-driven stories or emotional themes. However, I do not think that pattern is set in stone. A lot depends on upbringing, school environment, marketing, and individual temperament. Sometimes children are nudged in a certain direction simply because society expects them to like particular things. In other words, taste is often shaped, not hard-wired.
components.chinese: 有些男孩可能比較偏向動作、科幻，或者運動相關的書；而有些女孩則可能比較容易被以人物關係或情感主題為主的故事吸引。不過，我並不認為這種模式是鐵板一塊的。很多時候，影響因素其實包括成長背景、學校環境、市場包裝，以及個人性格。有時候，孩子之所以被推向某一類型，只是因為社會早已預設他們應該喜歡某些東西。換句話說，閱讀口味很多時候是被塑造出來的，而不是天生注定的。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Some boys may lean towards action, science fiction, or
sports-related books, while some girls may be more drawn to
relationship-driven stories or emotional themes. However, I do not think that
pattern is set in stone. A lot depends on upbringing, school environment,
marketing, and individual temperament. Sometimes children are nudged in a
certain direction simply because society expects them to like particular things.
In other words, taste is often shaped, not hard-wired.
[有些男孩可能比較偏向動作、科幻，或者運動相關的書；而有些女孩則可能比較容易
被以人物關係或情感主題為主的故事吸引。不過，我並不認為這種模式是鐵板一塊
的。很多時候，影響因素其實包括成長背景、學校環境、市場包裝，以及個人性格。有
時候，孩子之所以被推向某一類型，只是因為社會早已預設他們應該喜歡某些東西。
換句話說，閱讀口味很多時候是被塑造出來的，而不是天生注定的。]

components.english: For instance, a boy may love a quiet, emotional novel, and a girl may be crazy about action-packed fantasy. Once you look beyond the labels, it becomes clear that readers are not as easy to put into neat little boxes as people think.
components.chinese: 例如，一個男孩可能很喜歡安靜而感性的小說，而一個女孩也可能非常迷戀節奏緊湊、充滿動作場面的奇幻故事。只要你不再被那些標籤牽著走，就會明白，讀者其實並沒有想像中那麼容易被分進整整齊齊的小格子裡。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a boy may love a quiet, emotional novel, and a girl may
be crazy about action-packed fantasy. Once you look beyond the labels, it
becomes clear that readers are not as easy to put into neat little boxes as people
think.
[例如，一個男孩可能很喜歡安靜而感性的小說，而一個女孩也可能非常迷戀節奏緊
湊、充滿動作場面的奇幻故事。只要你不再被那些標籤牽著走，就會明白，讀者其實並
沒有想像中那麼容易被分進整整齊齊的小格子裡。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say boys and girls do share a lot of reading interests, even though certain trends may differ. What matters most in the end is not gender, but the individual reader’s personality, curiosity, and taste.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說男孩和女孩在閱讀興趣上其實有不少重疊，雖然某些傾向可能略有不同。但說到底，真正最重要的不是性別，而是每個讀者本身的個性、好奇心，以及閱讀口味。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say boys and girls do share a lot of reading interests,
even though certain trends may differ. What matters most in the end is not
gender, but the individual reader’s personality, curiosity, and taste.
[總結來說，我會說男孩和女孩在閱讀興趣上其實有不少重疊，雖然某些傾向可能略
有不同。但說到底，真正最重要的不是性別，而是每個讀者本身的個性、好奇心，以及
閱讀口味。]

question.english: What kind of books do people like to read in your country?
question.chinese: 你國家的人喜歡讀甚麼類型的書？

source_lines.text: Topic: What kind of books do people like to read in your country?
你國家的人喜歡讀甚麼類型的書？

components.english: In Hong Kong, I’d say people tend to like a mixture of practical books and entertaining fiction.
components.chinese: 在香港，我會說人們通常喜歡閱讀實用類書籍和娛樂性小說的混合。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In Hong Kong, I’d say people tend to like a mixture of practical books
and entertaining fiction.
[在香港，我會說人們通常喜歡閱讀實用類書籍和娛樂性小說的混合。]

components.english: Hong Kong is a fast-paced and highly competitive place, so many people are drawn to books that feel useful in everyday life. That includes self-improvement books, business books, language-learning materials, and anything related to career development or personal growth. At the same time, people also need an escape from the daily grind, so novels, thrillers, romance, and fantasy still have plenty of appeal. In other words, many readers want books that either help them move forward or help them switch off.
components.chinese: 香港是一個節奏很快、競爭也相當激烈的地方，所以很多人都會被那些感覺對日常生活有幫助的書吸引。這包括自我提升書籍、商業書籍、語言學習材料，以及任何和事業發展或個人成長有關的內容。與此同時，人們也需要從日常忙碌中抽離一下，所以小說、驚悚故事、愛情故事和奇幻作品仍然很有吸引力。換句話說，很多讀者想要的書，不是幫助自己向前走，就是幫助自己暫時放鬆下來。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Hong Kong is a fast-paced and highly competitive place, so
many people are drawn to books that feel useful in everyday life. That includes
self-improvement books, business books, language-learning materials, and
anything related to career development or personal growth. At the same time,
people also need an escape from the daily grind, so novels, thrillers, romance,
and fantasy still have plenty of appeal. In other words, many readers want books
that either help them move forward or help them switch off.
[香港是一個節奏很快、競爭也相當激烈的地方，所以很多人都會被那些感覺對日常生
活有幫助的書吸引。這包括自我提升書籍、商業書籍、語言學習材料，以及任何和事業
發展或個人成長有關的內容。與此同時，人們也需要從日常忙碌中抽離一下，所以小
說、驚悚故事、愛情故事和奇幻作品仍然很有吸引力。換句話說，很多讀者想要的書，
不是幫助自己向前走，就是幫助自己暫時放鬆下來。]

components.english: For example, a university student or a young professional may read books about productivity, communication, or finance, while the same person might also enjoy a crime novel or a romance book on the weekend. That kind of contrast is quite common, because reading often serves both the head and the heart.
components.chinese: 例如，一個大學生或年輕上班族可能會閱讀關於效率、溝通，或者理財的書，但同一個人到了週末，也可能會喜歡看推理小說或愛情小說。這種反差其實很常見，因為閱讀很多時候既是為了腦袋，也是為了內心。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a university student or a young professional may read
books about productivity, communication, or finance, while the same person
might also enjoy a crime novel or a romance book on the weekend. That kind of
contrast is quite common, because reading often serves both the head and the
heart.
[例如，一個大學生或年輕上班族可能會閱讀關於效率、溝通，或者理財的書，但同一
個人到了週末，也可能會喜歡看推理小說或愛情小說。這種反差其實很常見，因為閱
讀很多時候既是為了腦袋，也是為了內心。]

components.english: So, generally speaking, people in Hong Kong seem to enjoy books that are either practical and useful or engaging enough to help them unwind.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，香港人似乎特別喜歡兩類書：一類是實用而有幫助的，另一類則是足夠吸引人、能讓他們放鬆下來的。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, generally speaking, people in Hong Kong seem to enjoy books
that are either practical and useful or engaging enough to help them unwind.
[所以，整體來說，香港人似乎特別喜歡兩類書：一類是實用而有幫助的，另一類則是
足夠吸引人、能讓他們放鬆下來的。]

components.english: That said, reading preferences also differ quite a lot depending on age, background, and lifestyle.
components.chinese: 不過，閱讀口味其實也會因年齡、背景和生活方式而有頗大的差異。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, reading preferences also differ quite a lot depending on age,
background, and lifestyle.
[不過，閱讀口味其實也會因年齡、背景和生活方式而有頗大的差異。]

components.english: Younger readers are often more drawn to fantasy, young adult fiction, manga, graphic novels, or books that feel emotionally immediate. Older readers, by contrast, may lean more towards history, biographies, health-related books, or social commentary. There is also a strong place for children’s books, exam-related reading, and translated works, because Hong Kong is a city where East and West often meet on the same shelf. In a way, people’s bookshelves can tell you quite a lot about the rhythm of the city itself: ambitious, practical, but always looking for a little room to breathe.
components.chinese: 年輕讀者通常比較容易被奇幻小說、青少年文學、漫畫、圖像小說，或者情感上較直接的作品吸引。相比之下，年紀較大的讀者則可能更偏向歷史、傳記、健康類書籍，或者社會評論。除此之外，兒童讀物、與考試有關的閱讀材料，以及翻譯作品也有很重要的位置，因為香港是一個東西文化常常在同一個書架上相遇的城市。某程度上，人們的書架其實能說出很多關於這座城市節奏的事情：它有野心、很實際，但同時也一直在尋找一點可以喘息的空間。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Younger readers are often more drawn to fantasy, young adult
fiction, manga, graphic novels, or books that feel emotionally immediate. Older
readers, by contrast, may lean more towards history, biographies, health-related
books, or social commentary. There is also a strong place for children’s books,
exam-related reading, and translated works, because Hong Kong is a city where
East and West often meet on the same shelf. In a way, people’s bookshelves can
tell you quite a lot about the rhythm of the city itself: ambitious, practical, but
always looking for a little room to breathe.
[年輕讀者通常比較容易被奇幻小說、青少年文學、漫畫、圖像小說，或者情感上較直
接的作品吸引。相比之下，年紀較大的讀者則可能更偏向歷史、傳記、健康類書籍，或
者社會評論。除此之外，兒童讀物、與考試有關的閱讀材料，以及翻譯作品也有很重要
的位置，因為香港是一個東西文化常常在同一個書架上相遇的城市。某程度上，人們
的書架其實能說出很多關於這座城市節奏的事情：它有野心、很實際，但同時也一直
在尋找一點可以喘息的空間。]

components.english: For instance, parents may buy storybooks and educational books for their children, teenagers may enjoy manga or popular fiction, and working adults may pick up books on investment, psychology, or career advice. So, although tastes differ, the common thread is that people usually want reading to be either meaningful or enjoyable, and ideally both.
components.chinese: 例如，家長可能會為孩子買故事書和教育類書籍；青少年可能喜歡漫畫或流行小說；而工作的成年人則可能會選擇投資、心理學，或者職場建議方面的書。所以，雖然大家的口味不同，但其中一條共同主線就是：人們通常希望閱讀要麼有意義，要麼有樂趣，而最理想的情況當然是兩者兼備。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, parents may buy storybooks and educational books for
their children, teenagers may enjoy manga or popular fiction, and working
adults may pick up books on investment, psychology, or career advice. So,
although tastes differ, the common thread is that people usually want reading to
be either meaningful or enjoyable, and ideally both.
[例如，家長可能會為孩子買故事書和教育類書籍；青少年可能喜歡漫畫或流行小說；
而工作的成年人則可能會選擇投資、心理學，或者職場建議方面的書。所以，雖然大家
的口味不同，但其中一條共同主線就是：人們通常希望閱讀要麼有意義，要麼有樂趣，
而最理想的情況當然是兩者兼備。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people in Hong Kong read quite a wide range of books, but the most popular ones are usually either useful, entertaining, or emotionally relatable. If a book can do more than one of those things, it is likely to go down very well.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說香港人閱讀的書類型相當廣泛，但最受歡迎的，通常還是那些實用、有娛樂性，或者在情感上容易引起共鳴的書。如果一本書能同時做到不只一點，它往往就更容易受到歡迎。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people in Hong Kong read quite a wide range of
books, but the most popular ones are usually either useful, entertaining, or
emotionally relatable. If a book can do more than one of those things, it is likely
to go down very well.
[總結來說，我會說香港人閱讀的書類型相當廣泛，但最受歡迎的，通常還是那些實
用、有娛樂性，或者在情感上容易引起共鳴的書。如果一本書能同時做到不只一點，它
往往就更容易受到歡迎。]

question.english: In your opinion, do business people have to work long hours?
question.chinese: 你認為商界人士是否必須長時間工作？

source_lines.text: Topic: In your opinion, do business people have to work long hours?
你認為商界人士是否必須長時間工作？

components.english: In many cases, yes, I think business people do end up working long hours, especially if they are in leadership roles or highly competitive industries.
components.chinese: 在很多情況下，是的，我認為商界人士確實往往需要長時間工作，尤其當他們身處管理層，或者是在競爭非常激烈的行業裡。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In many cases, yes, I think business people do end up working long
hours, especially if they are in leadership roles or highly competitive industries.
[在很多情況下，是的，我認為商界人士確實往往需要長時間工作，尤其當他們身處管
理層，或者是在競爭非常激烈的行業裡。]

components.english: The main reason is that business work often comes with heavy responsibility, tight deadlines, client pressure, and constant problem-solving. If a company is trying to grow, stay ahead of competitors, or keep major clients happy, the workload can snowball very quickly. On top of that, senior people are usually the ones who have to make the final call when something goes wrong. In other words, when the stakes are high, the hours often stretch.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，商業工作往往伴隨著沉重責任、緊迫期限、客戶壓力，以及沒完沒了的解難工作。如果一家公司正努力發展、希望領先競爭對手，或者要維持重要客戶的滿意度，工作量很快就可能越滾越大。除此之外，當事情出問題時，通常還是高層要負責作最後決定。換句話說，當代價很高的時候，工作時間往往也會被拉長。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that business work often comes with heavy
responsibility, tight deadlines, client pressure, and constant problem-solving. If
a company is trying to grow, stay ahead of competitors, or keep major clients
happy, the workload can snowball very quickly. On top of that, senior people are
usually the ones who have to make the final call when something goes wrong. In
other words, when the stakes are high, the hours often stretch.
[主要原因是，商業工作往往伴隨著沉重責任、緊迫期限、客戶壓力，以及沒完沒了的
解難工作。如果一家公司正努力發展、希望領先競爭對手，或者要維持重要客戶的滿
意度，工作量很快就可能越滾越大。除此之外，當事情出問題時，通常還是高層要負責
作最後決定。換句話說，當代價很高的時候，工作時間往往也會被拉長。]

components.english: For example, a business owner launching a new product may have to deal with suppliers in the morning, clients in the afternoon, and urgent emails at night. In that kind of situation, they are not just doing one job; they are keeping several plates spinning at once.
components.chinese: 例如，一位正在推出新產品的老闆，可能早上要處理供應商問題，下午要見客戶，晚上還要回覆緊急電郵。在這種情況下，他其實不只是做一份工作，而是同時要讓好幾件事情都維持運轉。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a business owner launching a new product may have to
deal with suppliers in the morning, clients in the afternoon, and urgent emails
at night. In that kind of situation, they are not just doing one job; they are
keeping several plates spinning at once.
[例如，一位正在推出新產品的老闆，可能早上要處理供應商問題，下午要見客戶，晚
上還要回覆緊急電郵。在這種情況下，他其實不只是做一份工作，而是同時要讓好幾
件事情都維持運轉。]

components.english: So yes, I do think long hours are quite common in the business world, mainly because the pressure and responsibility can be enormous.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我的確認為長時間工作在商界是相當常見的，主要因為那裡的壓力和責任都可能非常巨大。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I do think long hours are quite common in the business
world, mainly because the pressure and responsibility can be enormous.
[所以，是的，我的確認為長時間工作在商界是相當常見的，主要因為那裡的壓力和責
任都可能非常巨大。]

components.english: That said, I do not think business people should have to work long hours all the time, because that is not always a sign of success or efficiency.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為商界人士應該一直都要長時間工作，因為那並不一定代表成功，也不一定代表高效率。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think business people should have to work long
hours all the time, because that is not always a sign of success or efficiency.
[不過，我並不認為商界人士應該一直都要長時間工作，因為那並不一定代表成功，也
不一定代表高效率。]

components.english: To be honest, working long hours can sometimes look impressive on the surface, but it may hide deeper problems underneath. If people are always burning the candle at both ends, they are more likely to make poor decisions, lose focus, and wear themselves thin. In the long run, smart systems, good delegation, and clear priorities matter more than simply staying at the office for endless hours. So, while long hours may come with the territory from time to time, they should not become a badge of honour.
components.chinese: 老實說，長時間工作有時表面上看起來好像很拼搏，但底下其實可能隱藏著更深層的問題。如果一個人總是兩頭燒，他就更容易作出錯誤判斷、失去專注，並把自己慢慢耗乾。從長遠來看，聰明的制度、良好的分工，以及清晰的優先次序，其實比單純在辦公室熬很久更重要。所以，雖然長工時有時的確是工作的一部分，但它不應該變成一種值得炫耀的標誌。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, working long hours can sometimes look
impressive on the surface, but it may hide deeper problems underneath. If
people are always burning the candle at both ends, they are more likely to make
poor decisions, lose focus, and wear themselves thin. In the long run, smart
systems, good delegation, and clear priorities matter more than simply staying
at the office for endless hours. So, while long hours may come with the territory
from time to time, they should not become a badge of honour.
[老實說，長時間工作有時表面上看起來好像很拼搏，但底下其實可能隱藏著更深層
的問題。如果一個人總是兩頭燒，他就更容易作出錯誤判斷、失去專注，並把自己慢慢
耗乾。從長遠來看，聰明的制度、良好的分工，以及清晰的優先次序，其實比單純在辦
公室熬很久更重要。所以，雖然長工時有時的確是工作的一部分，但它不應該變成一
種值得炫耀的標誌。]

components.english: For instance, a manager with a capable team and well-organised systems may finish work at a reasonable time and still deliver excellent results. By contrast, someone who works late every night may simply be stuck in a chaotic system rather than genuinely being more productive.
components.chinese: 例如，一位擁有能幹團隊和有條理制度的經理，可能可以在合理時間內下班，但仍然交出很出色的成果。相比之下，一個每天都工作到很晚的人，未必是真的更有效率，他也可能只是困在一個混亂的系統裡而已。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a manager with a capable team and well-organised
systems may finish work at a reasonable time and still deliver excellent results.
By contrast, someone who works late every night may simply be stuck in a
chaotic system rather than genuinely being more productive.
[例如，一位擁有能幹團隊和有條理制度的經理，可能可以在合理時間內下班，但仍然
交出很出色的成果。相比之下，一個每天都工作到很晚的人，未必是真的更有效率，他
也可能只是困在一個混亂的系統裡而已。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say business people often do work long hours, but they should not have to do so as a matter of course. At times it is unavoidable, but ideally success should come from working wisely, not just working endlessly.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說商界人士的確常常需要長時間工作，但那不應該被視為理所當然。有時那是無可避免的，不過理想情況下，成功應該來自聰明地工作，而不只是沒完沒了地工作。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say business people often do work long hours, but
they should not have to do so as a matter of course. At times it is unavoidable,
but ideally success should come from working wisely, not just working endlessly.
[總結來說，我會說商界人士的確常常需要長時間工作，但那不應該被視為理所當然。
有時那是無可避免的，不過理想情況下，成功應該來自聰明地工作，而不只是沒完沒
了地工作。]

question.english: How do business people relax?
question.chinese: 商界人士通常怎樣放鬆？

source_lines.text: Topic: How do business people relax?
商界人士通常怎樣放鬆？

components.english: I think many business people relax by doing things that help them switch off from work, such as exercising, travelling, socialising, or simply spending quiet time with family.
components.chinese: 我認為，很多商界人士會透過一些能讓自己從工作中抽離的事情來放鬆，例如做運動、旅行、社交，或者單純和家人安靜地相處。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think many business people relax by doing things that help them
switch off from work, such as exercising, travelling, socialising, or simply
spending quiet time with family.
[我認為，很多商界人士會透過一些能讓自己從工作中抽離的事情來放鬆，例如做運
動、旅行、社交，或者單純和家人安靜地相處。]

components.english: The reason is quite simple: business life can be intense, fast-moving, and mentally draining, so people need ways to clear their head and recharge their batteries. After spending the whole day making decisions, handling pressure, and putting out fires, many of them do not want more stimulation; they want relief. In that sense, relaxation is not a luxury for them. It is more like a pressure valve.
components.chinese: 原因其實很簡單：商界生活往往節奏很快、壓力很大，而且相當消耗心神，所以人們需要一些方法來清空腦袋，替自己重新充電。經過一整天不停作決定、承受壓力，以及處理各種突發問題之後，很多人並不想再受更多刺激；他們想要的是舒緩。從這個角度來看，放鬆對他們來說並不是奢侈品，而更像是一個洩壓閥。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is quite simple: business life can be intense,
fast-moving, and mentally draining, so people need ways to clear their head and
recharge their batteries. After spending the whole day making decisions,
handling pressure, and putting out fires, many of them do not want more
stimulation; they want relief. In that sense, relaxation is not a luxury for them.
It is more like a pressure valve.
[原因其實很簡單：商界生活往往節奏很快、壓力很大，而且相當消耗心神，所以人們
需要一些方法來清空腦袋，替自己重新充電。經過一整天不停作決定、承受壓力，以及
處理各種突發問題之後，很多人並不想再受更多刺激；他們想要的是舒緩。從這個角
度來看，放鬆對他們來說並不是奢侈品，而更像是一個洩壓閥。]

components.english: For example, some business people go to the gym after work, play golf at the weekend, or have dinner with close friends to take their mind off work. Even a short break like that can help them come back with a calmer mind and a fresher perspective.
components.chinese: 例如，有些商界人士會在下班後去健身、在週末打高爾夫球，或者和親密朋友吃晚飯，好讓自己暫時把工作拋開。即使只是這樣一段短短的休息，也能幫助他們以更平靜的心情和更清新的角度重新面對事情。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, some business people go to the gym after work, play
golf at the weekend, or have dinner with close friends to take their mind off
work. Even a short break like that can help them come back with a calmer mind
and a fresher perspective.
[例如，有些商界人士會在下班後去健身、在週末打高爾夫球，或者和親密朋友吃晚飯
，好讓自己暫時把工作拋開。即使只是這樣一段短短的休息，也能幫助他們以更平靜
的心情和更清新的角度重新面對事情。]

components.english: So, in general, I would say business people relax by stepping away from pressure and doing things that help them feel more human again.
components.chinese: 所以，一般來說，我會說商界人士放鬆的方法，就是先從壓力中抽身，然後做一些讓自己重新感覺像個「人」而不只是工作機器的事情。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in general, I would say business people relax by stepping away
from pressure and doing things that help them feel more human again.
[所以，一般來說，我會說商界人士放鬆的方法，就是先從壓力中抽身，然後做一些讓
自己重新感覺像個「人」而不只是工作機器的事情。]

components.english: That said, not all business people relax in loud or social ways; some prefer quieter habits, such as reading, listening to music, walking, or simply doing nothing for a while.
components.chinese: 不過，並不是所有商界人士都喜歡用熱鬧或社交的方式來放鬆；有些人反而偏好比較安靜的習慣，例如閱讀、聽音樂、散步，或者單純甚麼都不做一會兒。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, not all business people relax in loud or social ways; some
prefer quieter habits, such as reading, listening to music, walking, or simply
doing nothing for a while.
[不過，並不是所有商界人士都喜歡用熱鬧或社交的方式來放鬆；有些人反而偏好比較
安靜的習慣，例如閱讀、聽音樂、散步，或者單純甚麼都不做一會兒。]

components.english: I think that depends a lot on personality. Some people let their hair down by going out and being around others, whereas others relax by slowing the world right down. If someone’s job is full of meetings, phone calls, and constant noise, peace and quiet can feel like gold dust. Sometimes the best way to unwind is not to add something exciting, but to take something away.
components.chinese: 我認為這很大程度上取決於個人性格。有些人會透過外出和別人相處來盡情放鬆；但也有些人則是靠讓世界慢下來，才真正能夠休息。如果一個人的工作充滿會議、電話，以及無休止的嘈雜聲，那麼安靜和平靜對他來說就可能珍貴得像黃金一樣。有時最好的放鬆方法，不是再加進甚麼刺激的東西，而是把某些東西拿走。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: I think that depends a lot on personality. Some people let their
hair down by going out and being around others, whereas others relax by
slowing the world right down. If someone’s job is full of meetings, phone calls,
and constant noise, peace and quiet can feel like gold dust. Sometimes the best
way to unwind is not to add something exciting, but to take something away.
[我認為這很大程度上取決於個人性格。有些人會透過外出和別人相處來盡情放鬆；但
也有些人則是靠讓世界慢下來，才真正能夠休息。如果一個人的工作充滿會議、電話，
以及無休止的嘈雜聲，那麼安靜和平靜對他來說就可能珍貴得像黃金一樣。有時最好
的放鬆方法，不是再加進甚麼刺激的東西，而是把某些東西拿走。]

components.english: For instance, a manager who spends the whole day talking to clients may come home and relax by reading a novel, taking a long walk, or having a quiet cup of tea without checking their phone every five minutes. That kind of stillness can work wonders.
components.chinese: 例如，一位整天都在和客戶說話的經理，回到家後可能會透過看小說、散長步，或者安安靜靜喝一杯茶而不每五分鐘就看一次手機來放鬆。這種安靜下來的狀態，有時真的會帶來很大的效果。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a manager who spends the whole day talking to clients
may come home and relax by reading a novel, taking a long walk, or having a
quiet cup of tea without checking their phone every five minutes. That kind of
stillness can work wonders.
[例如，一位整天都在和客戶說話的經理，回到家後可能會透過看小說、散長步，或者
安安靜靜喝一杯茶而不每五分鐘就看一次手機來放鬆。這種安靜下來的狀態，有時真
的會帶來很大的效果。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say business people relax in different ways, but the common thread is that they need something that helps them draw a line between work and the rest of life. Whether that means exercise, family time, or simple silence, the goal is the same: to unwind and reset.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說商界人士放鬆的方法各有不同，但其中一條共同主線，就是他們都需要一些方式去在工作和生活其他部分之間劃出界線。無論那意味著運動、陪伴家人，還是單純的安靜，最終目的其實都是一樣的：讓自己鬆下來，重新調整狀態。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say business people relax in different ways, but the
common thread is that they need something that helps them draw a line
between work and the rest of life. Whether that means exercise, family time, or
simple silence, the goal is the same: to unwind and reset.
[總結來說，我會說商界人士放鬆的方法各有不同，但其中一條共同主線，就是他們都
需要一些方式去在工作和生活其他部分之間劃出界線。無論那意味著運動、陪伴家人
，還是單純的安靜，最終目的其實都是一樣的：讓自己鬆下來，重新調整狀態。]

question.english: How can a small business grow big?
question.chinese: 一間小型企業可以怎樣發展成大企業？

source_lines.text: Topic: How can a small business grow big?
一間小型企業可以怎樣發展成大企業？

components.english: I think a small business can grow big by first building a strong foundation, especially in terms of product quality, customer trust, and a clear identity.
components.chinese: 我認為，一間小型企業要發展成大企業，首先要建立穩固的基礎，特別是在產品質素、顧客信任，以及清晰定位這幾方面。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think a small business can grow big by first building a strong
foundation, especially in terms of product quality, customer trust, and a clear
identity.
[我認為，一間小型企業要發展成大企業，首先要建立穩固的基礎，特別是在產品質
素、顧客信任，以及清晰定位這幾方面。]

components.english: A lot of small businesses fail because they try to grow too fast before they are truly ready. In my view, growth built on weak foundations is like building a house on sand. If the product is unreliable, the service is inconsistent, or the business does not stand for anything clear, expansion can quickly become a headache rather than a success story. That is why the early stage matters so much. A business needs to know what it does well, who it serves, and why customers should come back.
components.chinese: 很多小型企業失敗，是因為它們在真正準備好之前就急著擴張。在我看來，建立在脆弱基礎上的增長，就像在沙地上蓋房子一樣。如果產品不可靠、服務不穩定，或者企業本身沒有清楚的定位，那麼擴張很快就會由一個成功故事變成一場麻煩。這就是為甚麼初期階段那麼重要。一門生意必須清楚知道自己最擅長甚麼、服務哪些人，以及為甚麼顧客值得再次回來。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: A lot of small businesses fail because they try to grow too fast
before they are truly ready. In my view, growth built on weak foundations is like
building a house on sand. If the product is unreliable, the service is inconsistent,
or the business does not stand for anything clear, expansion can quickly become
a headache rather than a success story. That is why the early stage matters so
much. A business needs to know what it does well, who it serves, and why
customers should come back.
[很多小型企業失敗，是因為它們在真正準備好之前就急著擴張。在我看來，建立在脆
弱基礎上的增長，就像在沙地上蓋房子一樣。如果產品不可靠、服務不穩定，或者企業
本身沒有清楚的定位，那麼擴張很快就會由一個成功故事變成一場麻煩。這就是為甚
麼初期階段那麼重要。一門生意必須清楚知道自己最擅長甚麼、服務哪些人，以及為
甚麼顧客值得再次回來。]

components.english: For example, a small café may begin by serving excellent coffee, offering warm service, and building a loyal base of regular customers. Once it earns a good name in the local area, it can gradually open a second branch or expand into delivery and branded products. In that case, growth happens brick by brick rather than all at once.
components.chinese: 例如，一間小咖啡店一開始可以先專注於提供高質素的咖啡、親切的服務，以及培養一批忠實熟客。當它在區內建立了良好口碑之後，就可以慢慢開第二間分店，或者發展外送服務和自家品牌產品。在這種情況下，增長是一磚一瓦慢慢累積出來的，而不是一下子衝上去。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a small café may begin by serving excellent coffee,
offering warm service, and building a loyal base of regular customers. Once it
earns a good name in the local area, it can gradually open a second branch or
expand into delivery and branded products. In that case, growth happens brick
by brick rather than all at once.
[例如，一間小咖啡店一開始可以先專注於提供高質素的咖啡、親切的服務，以及培養
一批忠實熟客。當它在區內建立了良好口碑之後，就可以慢慢開第二間分店，或者發
展外送服務和自家品牌產品。在這種情況下，增長是一磚一瓦慢慢累積出來的，而不
是一下子衝上去。]

components.english: So, to me, the first step towards becoming a big business is not chasing size blindly, but getting the basics right and earning trust over time.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，要成為大企業的第一步，並不是盲目追求規模，而是先把基本功做好，並且隨著時間贏得信任。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, to me, the first step towards becoming a big business is not
chasing size blindly, but getting the basics right and earning trust over time.
[所以，在我看來，要成為大企業的第一步，並不是盲目追求規模，而是先把基本功做
好，並且隨著時間贏得信任。]

components.english: Another key factor is learning how to scale wisely through good management, smart marketing, and the right people.
components.chinese: 另一個關鍵因素，是學會透過良好管理、聰明宣傳，以及合適的人才，去明智地擴張。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another key factor is learning how to scale wisely through good
management, smart marketing, and the right people.
[另一個關鍵因素，是學會透過良好管理、聰明宣傳，以及合適的人才，去明智地擴

components.english: Even a strong business cannot grow if everything depends on one person doing all the heavy lifting. At some point, the owner has to delegate, build systems, manage money carefully, and adapt to changes in the market. Social media, branding, customer feedback, and efficient operations all play an important role here. To be honest, growth is not just about working harder; it is about working smarter. A business that bites off more than it can chew may grow quickly on paper, but it can also come crashing down just as fast.
components.chinese: 即使一門生意本身很有實力，如果所有事情都只靠一個人苦苦支撐，它也很難真正做大。到了某個階段，老闆必須學會授權、建立制度、謹慎理財，並且適應市場變化。社交媒體、品牌建立、顧客意見，以及高效率運作，在這裡都扮演重要角色。老實說，成長不只是更努力地工作，而是更聰明地工作。一間企業如果一下子吞下超過自己能消化的東西，表面上也許會很快變大，但同樣也可能很快就垮下來。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Even a strong business cannot grow if everything depends on
one person doing all the heavy lifting. At some point, the owner has to delegate,
build systems, manage money carefully, and adapt to changes in the market.
Social media, branding, customer feedback, and efficient operations all play an
important role here. To be honest, growth is not just about working harder; it is
about working smarter. A business that bites off more than it can chew may
grow quickly on paper, but it can also come crashing down just as fast.
[即使一門生意本身很有實力，如果所有事情都只靠一個人苦苦支撐，它也很難真正
做大。到了某個階段，老闆必須學會授權、建立制度、謹慎理財，並且適應市場變化。
社交媒體、品牌建立、顧客意見，以及高效率運作，在這裡都扮演重要角色。老實說，
成長不只是更努力地工作，而是更聰明地工作。一間企業如果一下子吞下超過自己能
消化的東西，表面上也許會很快變大，但同樣也可能很快就垮下來。]

components.english: For instance, a small online clothing brand may start by selling through social media, then gradually improve its website, hire a small team, strengthen its delivery system, and use customer data to understand what sells best. Step by step, it can move from being a one-person operation to becoming a recognised brand.
components.chinese: 例如，一個小型網上服裝品牌可能一開始只是透過社交媒體賣貨，之後慢慢改善網站、聘請小團隊、強化物流安排，並利用顧客數據去了解甚麼產品最受歡迎。一步一步地，它就可以由一人經營的小生意，發展成一個有知名度的品牌。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a small online clothing brand may start by selling
through social media, then gradually improve its website, hire a small team,
strengthen its delivery system, and use customer data to understand what sells
best. Step by step, it can move from being a one-person operation to becoming a
recognised brand.
[例如，一個小型網上服裝品牌可能一開始只是透過社交媒體賣貨，之後慢慢改善網
站、聘請小團隊、強化物流安排，並利用顧客數據去了解甚麼產品最受歡迎。一步一步
地，它就可以由一人經營的小生意，發展成一個有知名度的品牌。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say a small business grows big by combining a solid foundation with smart expansion. In most cases, success does not happen overnight; it grows steadily through quality, trust, strategy, and patience.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說一間小型企業之所以能做大，是因為它把穩固基礎和聰明擴張結合起來。在大多數情況下，成功並不是一夜之間發生的；它是靠質素、信任、策略，以及耐心，一步一步長出來的。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say a small business grows big by combining a solid
foundation with smart expansion. In most cases, success does not happen
overnight; it grows steadily through quality, trust, strategy, and patience.
